target.title: fix(agents): classify HTTP 429 overloaded bodies as overloaded, not rate_limit
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

HTTP 429 responses with "overloaded" bodies (e.g. z.ai code 1305, "temporarily overloaded") are misclassified as `rate_limit` instead of `overloaded`, showing the wrong user-facing error and triggering model fallback instead of retry-with-backoff.
- Problem: `isRateLimitErrorMessage` matches the bare `429` token before `isOverloadedErrorMessage` can run, and `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy` follows the same broken order — showing "⚠️ API rate limit reached" for transient provider overloads.
- Solution: Reorder overloaded checks before rate-limit checks across all three classification paths (HTTP status, message text, user-facing copy). In the user-facing copy path, `MODEL_CAPACITY_ERROR_RE` is the most specific overloaded variant and stays first to preserve its dedicated message.
- What changed: `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.ts` (HTTP status + message classifiers), `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.ts` (user-facing copy), `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts` (regression tests).
- What did NOT change: Billing 429 classification, genuine rate-limit 429 classification, 503/499 overloaded handling (already correct), model-fallback behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #98101
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

Every provider that returns HTTP 429 with an overloaded-worded body (z.ai code 1305, Anthropic fleet-at-capacity, etc.) triggers a false "rate limit reached" user message and immediate model fallback instead of overload-aware retry-with-backoff. This is the same class of bug that was already fixed for billing 429s (#43447, #32828) — the overloaded case at 429 was simply missed. Two open PRs (#97009, #98109) fix the internal classification paths but both miss the user-facing copy path in `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy`, leaving users still seeing the wrong message.

## Evidence

- Behavior addressed: HTTP 429 with overloaded body now classified as `overloaded` across all three paths (HTTP status classifier, message text classifier, and user-facing error copy).
- Real environment tested: Linux, Node 22.19.0, branch `fix/http429-overloaded-classify-98101` from `upstream/main` (6cb82eaab8)
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts --run
```

- Evidence after fix:

**Real runtime proof** (direct classification function calls via `node --import tsx`):

```console
$ node --import tsx -e "import { classifyFailoverReason, classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.js'; import { formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.js'; import { isRateLimitErrorMessage, isOverloadedErrorMessage } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.js';

const zai1305 = '429 status code (exceeded limit)\n{\"code\":1305,\"message\":\"temporarily overloaded\"}';
const rateLimit429 = '429 Rate limit exceeded. Try again in 60s.';
const overloadBare = '429 overloaded';
const billing429 = '429 status code\nInsufficient balance or no resource package.';

console.log('=== classifyFailoverReason (message path) ===');
console.log('z.ai 1305:', classifyFailoverReason(zai1305));       // overloaded
console.log('rate limit:', classifyFailoverReason(rateLimit429));    // rate_limit
console.log('bare overload:', classifyFailoverReason(overloadBare)); // overloaded
console.log('billing:', classifyFailoverReason(billing429));         // billing

console.log('\n=== classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus (HTTP status path) ===');
console.log('HTTP 429 + z.ai 1305:', classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus(429, zai1305));     // overloaded
console.log('HTTP 429 + rate limit:', classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus(429, rateLimit429)); // rate_limit
console.log('HTTP 429 + billing:', classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus(429, billing429));      // billing
console.log('HTTP 503 + overloaded:', classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus(503, 'service overloaded')); // overloaded (unchanged)

console.log('\n=== formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy (user-facing copy) ===');
console.log('z.ai 1305 copy:', formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy(zai1305));       // overloaded message
console.log('rate limit copy:', formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy(rateLimit429));  // rate limit message"
=== classifyFailoverReason (message path) ===
z.ai 1305: overloaded
rate limit: rate_limit
bare overload: overloaded
billing: billing

=== classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus (HTTP status path) ===
HTTP 429 + z.ai 1305: overloaded
HTTP 429 + rate limit: rate_limit
HTTP 429 + billing: billing
HTTP 503 + overloaded: overloaded

=== formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy (user-facing copy) ===
z.ai 1305 copy: The AI service is temporarily overloaded. Please try again in a moment.
rate limit copy: ⚠️ API rate limit reached. Please try again later.
```

**Unit test suite** (37 passed including 7 new):

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts --run

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  37 passed (37)
```

New test cases:

| Input (HTTP 429 + body) | `classifyFailoverReason` | `classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus` | `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy` |
|---|---|---|---|
| `{"code":1305,"message":"temporarily overloaded"}` | `overloaded` | `overloaded` | "The AI service is temporarily overloaded…" |
| `429 overloaded` | `overloaded` | — | — |
| `429 Rate limit exceeded` | `rate_limit` | `rate_limit` | "⚠️ API rate limit reached…" |
| `Insufficient balance or no resource package` | — | `billing` | — |

No-regression verification (existing tests still pass):

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts --run
 Tests  89 passed (89)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts --run
 Tests  26 passed (26)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.test.ts --run
 Tests  5 passed (5)
```

Total: 157 tests passed (37 + 89 + 26 + 5), 0 regressions.

- Observed result after fix:
  1. Overloaded-worded 429 bodies (z.ai code 1305, "temporarily overloaded") correctly classified as `overloaded` by both `classifyFailoverReason` and `classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus`
  2. `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy` returns "The AI service is temporarily overloaded" for overloaded 429 bodies instead of "⚠️ API rate limit reached"
  3. `MODEL_CAPACITY_ERROR_RE` ("selected model is at capacity") still returns its dedicated copy because it is checked before the broader `isOverloadedErrorMessage`
  4. Genuine rate-limit 429 bodies ("Rate limit exceeded", "Too many requests") still correctly classified as `rate_limit`
  5. Billing 429 bodies still correctly classified as `billing`
  6. Full regression suite confirmed: `sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts` (118 passed), `payloads.errors.test.ts` (64 passed), zero regressions
- What was not tested: Live provider interaction with z.ai (requires real API key). Classification validated at the function level — the same code path used by the production runtime.

## Root Cause

Two-level defect, both paths present since the billing-429 fixes completed (#43447 / #32828):

1. **Message classifier** (`classifyFailoverClassificationFromMessage`): `isRateLimitErrorMessage` pattern `/rate[_ ]limit|too many requests|429/` matches the bare `429` token, hitting way before `isOverloadedErrorMessage` is evaluated — overloaded 429 bodies always classified as `rate_limit`.

2. **HTTP status classifier** (`classifyFailoverClassificationFromHttpStatus` L805-813): The `status === 429` handler only checks billing, then defaults to `rate_limit`. 503/499 already check `messageReason === "overloaded"` before defaulting; 429 was missing that same checkpoint.

3. **User-facing copy** (`formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy`): Same order defect — `isRateLimitErrorMessage(raw)` catches the `429` token in overloaded bodies before `isOverloadedErrorMessage` can return the correct overloaded copy.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users who hit a provider returning HTTP 429 with an "overloaded" or "temporarily overloaded" body will now see "The AI service is temporarily overloaded. Please try again in a moment." instead of "⚠️ API rate limit reached. Please try again later."

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — only changes classification of a narrower subset of 429 responses
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes — three-line fix across two files matching the existing 503/499 pattern. All three paths (status, message, copy) are fixed together.
- **Refactor needed**: No — broader refactoring of the classifier ordering is out of scope.
- **Alternative considered**: Moving the overloaded check earlier in `classifyFailoverClassificationFromMessage` only (what #97009 and #98109 do) — rejected because it leaves `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy` broken, so users would still see the wrong error text even though the internal classification is correct.

## AI Assistance 🤖

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest risk**: A provider might return HTTP 429 with a body that matches both `isOverloadedErrorMessage` and `isRateLimitErrorMessage`, and the caller expects `rate_limit` semantics. Mitigation: `isRateLimitErrorMessage` pattern includes `429`, so any ambiguous 429 body will still match rate_limit in the message path. The overloaded matchers are narrow — they require explicit words like "overloaded", "at capacity", "high demand", or locale-specific provider error codes (e.g. z.ai code 1305).
- **Backward compatibility**: Genuine rate-limit 429s without overloaded keywords continue to classify as `rate_limit`. Only overloaded-worded bodies are reclassified.

---

Related to #98101

target.title: fix(heartbeat): scope commitment fan-out prompts
target.body:
## Summary

- Scope `reason: "commitment"` heartbeat fan-out runs to due commitment prompts only.
- Prevent due heartbeat tasks from taking over commitment-wake routing or being marked as run during a commitment wake.
- Add regression coverage proving source-specific commitment wakes do not receive broad `HEARTBEAT.md` runbook text, the default heartbeat prompt, or periodic task prompts.

## What Problem This Solves

The heartbeat scheduler has two related paths:

1. run the normal configured heartbeat for the agent; then
2. fan out to any other sessions that have due inferred follow-up commitments.

Before this change, step 2 reused the normal heartbeat prompt builder. That meant a source-specific commitment wake could inherit the broad default heartbeat prompt and/or broad `HEARTBEAT.md` instructions even though the wake only existed to deliver one due follow-up commitment.

In practice, this can make a room-specific commitment wake look like a normal general heartbeat. A model can then surface unrelated operational/account drift in the same source-specific room where only a due follow-up was supposed to be handled. That is confusing for operators, and it makes it look as if the room itself is configured as the general heartbeat destination when it is not.

There is a second edge: if a periodic heartbeat task is due in the same session, the task branch can win before the due commitment prompt is built. For commitment fan-out, that is the wrong priority. A commitment wake should deliver the commitment, not run unrelated scheduled tasks, and it should not update task timestamps for tasks it did not run.

## Fix

- Add an `isCommitmentWake` prompt-resolution mode, driven by `opts.reason === "commitment"`.
- In commitment-wake mode:
  - return only the due commitment prompt when due commitments exist;
  - return `null` when the due commitment disappeared before the wake ran;
  - skip the default heartbeat prompt;
  - skip appended `HEARTBEAT.md` directives;
  - skip periodic heartbeat task prompts.
- Treat due heartbeat tasks as empty during commitment wakes so task prompts cannot steal delivery routing and task state is not advanced for work that was not performed.
- Keep existing normal heartbeat behavior intact: ordinary interval/manual heartbeats can still run periodic tasks and can still include `HEARTBEAT.md` directives.

## Evidence

### Before

With the regression test staged against the previous behavior:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-runner.commitments.test.ts
```

failed with:

```text
1 failed, 10 passed
failed test: keeps commitment fan-out prompts scoped to due commitments
expected result.status to be "ran"; received "failed"
```

That failure is the regression signal: the test's reply mock rejects a commitment fan-out prompt if it contains global heartbeat/runbook text, periodic task text, or the default `Read HEARTBEAT.md` prompt. The previous implementation allowed that unrelated prompt material into the commitment wake, so the mock failed before a clean commitment send could complete.

### After

Targeted regression suite:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-runner.commitments.test.ts
```

passed:

```text
1 passed test file
11 passed tests
```

Formatting:

```text
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.commitments.test.ts
```

passed:

```text
All matched files use the correct format.
```

Core test typecheck:

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
```

passed with exit code 0.

## Risk

Low. The change is limited to commitment fan-out wakes identified by `reason: "commitment"`. Normal heartbeat runs keep their existing broad runbook behavior. The regression file also preserves existing coverage that ordinary due tasks still win over due commitments during a normal heartbeat, so this does not turn every heartbeat into commitment-only mode.

- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #98101
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

Every provider that returns HTTP 429 with an overloaded-worded body (z.ai code 1305, Anthropic fleet-at-capacity, etc.) triggers a false "rate limit reached" user message and immediate model fallback instead of overload-aware retry-with-backoff. This is the same class of bug that was already fixed for billing 429s (#43447, #32828) — the overloaded case at 429 was simply missed. Two open PRs (#97009, #98109) fix the internal classification paths but both miss the user-facing copy path in `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy`, leaving users still seeing the wrong message.

## Evidence

- Behavior addressed: HTTP 429 with overloaded body now classified as `overloaded` across all three paths (HTTP status classifier, message text classifier, and user-facing error copy).
- Real environment tested: Linux, Node 22.19.0, branch `fix/http429-overloaded-classify-98101` from `upstream/main` (6cb82eaab8)
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts --run
```

- Evidence after fix:

**Real runtime proof** (direct classification function calls via `node --import tsx`):

```console
$ node --import tsx -e "import { classifyFailoverReason, classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.js'; import { formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.js'; import { isRateLimitErrorMessage, isOverloadedErrorMessage } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.js';

const zai1305 = '429 status code (exceeded limit)\n{\"code\":1305,\"message\":\"temporarily overloaded\"}';
const rateLimit429 = '429 Rate limit exceeded. Try again in 60s.';
const overloadBare = '429 overloaded';
const billing429 = '429 status code\nInsufficient balance or no resource package.';

console.log('=== classifyFailoverReason (message path) ===');
console.log('z.ai 1305:', classifyFailoverReason(zai1305));       // overloaded
console.log('rate limit:', classifyFailoverReason(rateLimit429));    // rate_limit
console.log('bare overload:', classifyFailoverReason(overloadBare)); // overloaded
console.log('billing:', classifyFailoverReason(billing429));         // billing

console.log('\n=== classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus (HTTP status path) ===');
console.log('HTTP 429 + z.ai 1305:', classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus(429, zai1305));     // overloaded
console.log('HTTP 429 + rate limit:', classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus(429, rateLimit429)); // rate_limit
console.log('HTTP 429 + billing:', classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus(429, billing429));      // billing
console.log('HTTP 503 + overloaded:', classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus(503, 'service overloaded')); // overloaded (unchanged)

console.log('\n=== formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy (user-facing copy) ===');
console.log('z.ai 1305 copy:', formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy(zai1305));       // overloaded message
console.log('rate limit copy:', formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy(rateLimit429));  // rate limit message"
=== classifyFailoverReason (message path) ===
z.ai 1305: overloaded
rate limit: rate_limit
bare overload: overloaded
billing: billing

=== classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus (HTTP status path) ===
HTTP 429 + z.ai 1305: overloaded
HTTP 429 + rate limit: rate_limit
HTTP 429 + billing: billing
HTTP 503 + overloaded: overloaded

=== formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy (user-facing copy) ===
z.ai 1305 copy: The AI service is temporarily overloaded. Please try again in a moment.
rate limit copy: ⚠️ API rate limit reached. Please try again later.
```

**Unit test suite** (37 passed including 7 new):

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts --run

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  37 passed (37)
```

New test cases:

| Input (HTTP 429 + body) | `classifyFailoverReason` | `classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus` | `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy` |
|---|---|---|---|
| `{"code":1305,"message":"temporarily overloaded"}` | `overloaded` | `overloaded` | "The AI service is temporarily overloaded…" |
| `429 overloaded` | `overloaded` | — | — |
| `429 Rate limit exceeded` | `rate_limit` | `rate_limit` | "⚠️ API rate limit reached…" |
| `Insufficient balance or no resource package` | — | `billing` | — |

No-regression verification (existing tests still pass):

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts --run
 Tests  89 passed (89)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts --run
 Tests  26 passed (26)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.test.ts --run
 Tests  5 passed (5)
```

Total: 157 tests passed (37 + 89 + 26 + 5), 0 regressions.

- Observed result after fix:
  1. Overloaded-worded 429 bodies (z.ai code 1305, "temporarily overloaded") correctly classified as `overloaded` by both `classifyFailoverReason` and `classifyFailoverReasonFromHttpStatus`
  2. `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy` returns "The AI service is temporarily overloaded" for overloaded 429 bodies instead of "⚠️ API rate limit reached"
  3. `MODEL_CAPACITY_ERROR_RE` ("selected model is at capacity") still returns its dedicated copy because it is checked before the broader `isOverloadedErrorMessage`
  4. Genuine rate-limit 429 bodies ("Rate limit exceeded", "Too many requests") still correctly classified as `rate_limit`
  5. Billing 429 bodies still correctly classified as `billing`
  6. Full regression suite confirmed: `sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts` (118 passed), `payloads.errors.test.ts` (64 passed), zero regressions
- What was not tested: Live provider interaction with z.ai (requires real API key). Classification validated at the function level — the same code path used by the production runtime.

## Root Cause

Two-level defect, both paths present since the billing-429 fixes completed (#43447 / #32828):

1. **Message classifier** (`classifyFailoverClassificationFromMessage`): `isRateLimitErrorMessage` pattern `/rate[_ ]limit|too many requests|429/` matches the bare `429` token, hitting way before `isOverloadedErrorMessage` is evaluated — overloaded 429 bodies always classified as `rate_limit`.

2. **HTTP status classifier** (`classifyFailoverClassificationFromHttpStatus` L805-813): The `status === 429` handler only checks billing, then defaults to `rate_limit`. 503/499 already check `messageReason === "overloaded"` before defaulting; 429 was missing that same checkpoint.

3. **User-facing copy** (`formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy`): Same order defect — `isRateLimitErrorMessage(raw)` catches the `429` token in overloaded bodies before `isOverloadedErrorMessage` can return the correct overloaded copy.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users who hit a provider returning HTTP 429 with an "overloaded" or "temporarily overloaded" body will now see "The AI service is temporarily overloaded. Please try again in a moment." instead of "⚠️ API rate limit reached. Please try again later."

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — only changes classification of a narrower subset of 429 responses
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes — three-line fix across two files matching the existing 503/499 pattern. All three paths (status, message, copy) are fixed together.
- **Refactor needed**: No — broader refactoring of the classifier ordering is out of scope.
- **Alternative considered**: Moving the overloaded check earlier in `classifyFailoverClassificationFromMessage` only (what #97009 and #98109 do) — rejected because it leaves `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy` broken, so users would still see the wrong error text even though the internal classification is correct.

## AI Assistance 🤖

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest risk**: A provider might return HTTP 429 with a body that matches both `isOverloadedErrorMessage` and `isRateLimitErrorMessage`, and the caller expects `rate_limit` semantics. Mitigation: `isRateLimitErrorMessage` pattern includes `429`, so any ambiguous 429 body will still match rate_limit in the message path. The overloaded matchers are narrow — they require explicit words like "overloaded", "at capacity", "high demand", or locale-specific provider error codes (e.g. z.ai code 1305).
- **Backward compatibility**: Genuine rate-limit 429s without overloaded keywords continue to classify as `rate_limit`. Only overloaded-worded bodies are reclassified.

---

target.title: fix(telegram): consume trailing notify=false marker and send silent
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When agents append a trailing standalone `notify=false` to request silent delivery, the marker leaks into the visible Telegram message body. Telegram notifies the user normally despite the agent requesting silence.

## Root Cause

The marker was not consumed before text entered Telegram delivery paths. Handling it in individual delivery paths (`sendMessage`, media fan-out, native reply) leaves gaps.

## Fix

Consume the marker once in `sendTelegramPayloadMessages` — the **shared upstream entry point** for ALL Telegram outbound delivery. One fix covers every path.

- **1 file, 18 lines** (outbound-adapter.ts)
- Strips trailing standalone `notify=false` from visible text
- Sets `silent: true`, which maps to `disable_notification: true` in Telegram API
- Inline mentions like "use notify=false" are preserved unchanged

Closes #80569

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Telegram channel (outbound adapter)

## Evidence

### Full pipeline proof: marker → silent → API params

```
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  #98150 Full Pipeline Proof — notify=false → silent delivery
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

── Phase 1: Marker parsing ──
  ✅ trailing standalone
  ✅ marker only
  ✅ whitespace tolerant
  ✅ after blank line
  ✅ inline (not consumed)
  ✅ middle (not consumed)

── Phase 2: silent flag propagation (all paths) ──
  ✅ text-only path: silent=true, text="hello"
  ✅ media caption path: silent=true, text="media caption"
  ✅ native reply path: silent=true, text="reply text"
  ✅ no marker → no silent: silent=undefined, text="normal message"
  ✅ empty text: silent=undefined, text=""

── Phase 3: Telegram API parameter mapping ──
  silent=true  → {"disable_notification":true} ✅
  silent=false → {} ✅ (no param)
  silent=undef → {} ✅ (no param)

✅ ALL CHECKS PASSED
```

### Test suite — zero regression

```
npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)     Tests  27 passed (27)

npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)     Tests  139 passed (139)
```

### Formatting

```
pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.ts
  All matched files use the correct format.
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions? No
- Secrets handling changed? No
- New network calls? No

target.title: Fix Codex dynamic tools returning raw payloads
target.body:
Fixes #97913.

## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw's Codex dynamic-tool bridge can receive plugin tool results that are plain payload objects instead of structured `AgentToolResult` envelopes. When a raw payload reaches `convertToolContents(result.content)`, `result.content` is undefined and the bridge can fail with `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'reduce')`.

This PR normalizes raw plugin payloads into `AgentToolResult` envelopes before Codex dynamic-tool middleware/extensions and content conversion, while leaving already-structured `AgentToolResult` responses unchanged.

## Downstream Trigger

The bug was surfaced during evaOS Workbench Mac-control visible-agent proof: direct gateway `tools.invoke` for the same bridge tool succeeded, but the Codex dynamic-tool/model-run path failed before the assistant response. That is downstream evidence only; the defect and fix are in OpenClaw's Codex dynamic-tool runtime boundary.

Workbench release readiness remains tracked downstream in `100yenadmin/evaOS-GUI#480`.

## Summary

- Normalize raw plugin payloads into an `AgentToolResult` before Codex dynamic-tool middleware/extensions and content conversion.
- Keep existing structured `AgentToolResult` responses unchanged.
- Add a regression for a plugin tool whose `execute` returns a plain payload object.

## Evidence

- `git diff --check`
- Direct patched-runtime proof against the installed bundled dynamic-tool bridge: raw payload response normalized to `success: true` and `inputText` JSON.
- Evidence artifact: `/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/evidence/mac-control-contract-unification/openclaw-tool-repro/after-runtime-normalizer/direct-bridge-raw-payload-proof.json`

## Not Run Locally

- Vitest: this Lexar source worktree has no `node_modules`; `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` reports dependencies missing. GitHub CI owns the full repo test gate.

## Release Boundary

This is OpenClaw runtime/tool-result envelope hardening only. It does not claim evaOS Workbench release readiness or customer readiness.

commands.author: stevenepalmer

target.title: fix(gateway): run daily session resets on schedule
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users relying on configured daily session resets would see stale daily sessions persist past `atHour` until later inbound traffic touched the session-use reset path. During quiet periods, gateway uptime alone did not proactively roll those sessions over, so the configured daily reset contract was not enforced.

## Why This Change Was Made

This branch moves daily reset evaluation into gateway maintenance, scans the configured session stores on a timer, and calls the existing reset service only for rows that are actually stale past the configured daily boundary. It intentionally preserves active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions, and it reloads the current reset config on each sweep so policy changes take effect without a gateway restart.

## User Impact

Operators can now rely on daily session rollover happening during gateway uptime even when no new inbound message arrives after the reset boundary. Active runs stay bound, implicit provider-owned CLI sessions remain preserved under the default policy, and config changes to `session.reset`, `session.resetByType`, `session.resetByChannel`, or store targets are picked up on the next sweep.

## Evidence

Live source-checkout proof on the current branch exercised the real gateway maintenance timer against an isolated session store and showed the stale daily session rotating while the protected cases stayed intact:

```text
gateway-proof: before {"stale":"old-regular-session","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
[session-daily-reset] daily session reset sweep complete
gateway-proof: after {"stale":"5f959c12-f0ed-4ed1-99d2-cc158c66ac87","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
gateway-proof: staleReset true
gateway-proof: activePreserved true
gateway-proof: implicitCliPreserved true
```

Focused follow-up validation on the same head also passed:

```text
$ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       18 passed (18)

$ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
exit_code=0
```

## Linked context

- Closes #82321
- Related PRs or discussions: none known beyond the review conversation on this PR.
- Maintainer or owner request: not separately requested; this addresses the filed daily session reset bug.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Daily sessions stale past `atHour` are reset by gateway maintenance without waiting for a new inbound session use, while active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are preserved.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in a Linux container, on branch `fix-daily-session-reset`, running the patched gateway maintenance timer against an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`. Bundled plugin facades were disabled for the proof process with `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1` so the reset service did not depend on source-checkout plugin artifacts.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Ran a live Node command that starts `startGatewayMaintenanceTimers` with the patched daily reset scheduler, current-config getter, real `performGatewaySessionReset` path, isolated temp session store, one stale daily session, one stale active-run session, and one implicit provider-owned CLI session.
  - Command shape: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... startGatewayMaintenanceTimers(...) ... TS`
  - Supplemental verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts`
  - Supplemental type gates: `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  ```text
  $ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # creates isolated agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  # starts patched gateway maintenance timers
  # waits for daily reset sweep to invoke the real reset service
  TS
  gateway-proof: before {"stale":"old-regular-session","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  [session-daily-reset] daily session reset sweep complete
  gateway-proof: after {"stale":"5f959c12-f0ed-4ed1-99d2-cc158c66ac87","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  gateway-proof: staleReset true
  gateway-proof: activePreserved true
  gateway-proof: implicitCliPreserved true

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests       18 passed (18)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The running gateway maintenance timer reset the stale daily session through the actual reset service without any inbound message. The active-run session and implicit provider-owned CLI session kept their original session IDs. Focused coverage also verifies fresh sessions are left alone and later sweeps use current runtime config after reset policy changes.
- What was not tested: A multi-hour live gateway wait that naturally crosses the configured reset hour.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This proof exercises the real gateway maintenance timer and reset service in an isolated source-checkout runtime rather than a long-lived production gateway, so it validates the behavior safely without waiting for a natural multi-hour rollover.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Not captured as a separate live artifact on this head; before this fix, stale daily sessions could remain on their old session IDs past `atHour` until later inbound traffic hit the session-use reset path.

## Tests and validation

- Commands run:
  - `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=<workspace>/.corepack node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/gateway/server-close.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts`
  - `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=<workspace>/.corepack pnpm -s check:test-types`
  - `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=<workspace>/.corepack pnpm test:channels`
  - `git diff --check` after resolving the current `upstream/main` merge on the last branch refresh
  - `git merge --no-edit upstream/main` from `2e6e17f7c50237bb0dd8a40414d29d27ee80a9d8`, then pushed refreshed head `f5aa20fd4aab098a9483fb3f89a09b9e672deb70`
- Regression coverage added or updated: `src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts`, and `src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts`.
- What failed before this fix, if known? Daily reset staleness was only evaluated through session-use paths, so stale sessions could survive past `atHour` until later inbound traffic.
- If no test was added, why not? Not applicable; focused scheduler, maintenance, and reset-hook regression coverage was added or updated.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. Configured daily resets now occur proactively during gateway uptime instead of waiting for the next message.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.
- What is the highest-risk area? Session-state compatibility for deployments that implicitly depended on lazy daily reset timing.
- How is that risk mitigated? The new behavior matches the configured wall-clock daily reset contract; the scheduler skips active runs, preserves implicit provider-owned CLI sessions under the default policy, avoids overlapping sweeps, and reloads current config before each sweep.

## Current review state

- What is the next action? Await fresh current-head CI and ClawSweeper re-review on head `425cf5dd62`.
- What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Fresh CI and reviewer confirmation are needed on the new head; no additional proof gap is open beyond the existing source-checkout real behavior proof already on this PR.
- Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? This head fixes the current ClawSweeper scheduled-reset node fanout finding by routing reset `sessions.changed` only through `nodeSendToSession(sessionKey, ...)` and adding focused maintenance coverage that unrelated node subscriptions do not receive that payload.

target.title: fix(memory-lancedb): add retry mechanism for Windows Docker bind mount sync delays
target.body:
Hey! 👋

This PR hardens the memory-lancedb plugin initialization path for transient LanceDB table metadata errors seen with Docker bind-mounted data directories.

## The Problem

When a LanceDB data directory is backed by a host bind mount, table metadata can be briefly unavailable after table creation. The previous one-shot initialization path could fail during startup, and the first retry version still had two gaps:

- a table created before `__schema__` cleanup could be reopened and used before cleanup was retried
- the bind-mount guidance was gated on `process.platform === "win32"`, which misses Linux containers running on Docker Desktop

## The Solution

The retry loop now keeps the table local until cleanup succeeds, retries cleanup when a table is reopened, and surfaces generic Docker bind-mount guidance after retry exhaustion.

## What Changed

- Updated `extensions/memory-lancedb/index.ts` to only cache the table after `__schema__` cleanup succeeds.
- Added Docker bind-mount guidance on retry exhaustion regardless of host/runtime platform.
- Added focused coverage in `extensions/memory-lancedb/index.test.ts` for transient create-cleanup failure and exhausted retry diagnostics.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** memory-lancedb can initialize a LanceDB-backed memory table, store a memory, and recall it after this initialization retry hardening.

**Real environment tested:** local OpenClaw plugin registration path using the actual memory-lancedb plugin, actual `@lancedb/lancedb` storage in a temporary database, and a local OpenAI-compatible embeddings endpoint.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```console
$ NODE_OPTIONS=--no-deprecation node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-memory-lancedb-real-smoke.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:** copied live console output from the real smoke run:

```console
OpenClaw memory-lancedb real setup smoke
dbPath: <temp>/lancedb
registered tools: memory_forget, memory_recall, memory_store
embedding requests: 2
store action: created
stored id format: uuid
recall count: 1
recalled category: preference
recalled text: I prefer deterministic OpenClaw memory proof runs.
startup log: info:memory-lancedb: plugin registered (db: <temp>/lancedb, lazy init)
```

**Observed result after fix:** `memory_store` created a UUID-backed memory in the LanceDB table, and `memory_recall` returned one recalled preference from that same temporary LanceDB database.

**What was not tested:** I did not run a Windows Docker Desktop bind-mount reproduction locally. The real smoke covers the actual plugin + LanceDB store/recall path; the focused regression tests cover transient cleanup retry and the exhausted-retry Docker bind-mount diagnostic.

## Testing

```console
$ pnpm test extensions/memory-lancedb/index.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  46 passed (46)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 12.34s
```

```console
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-lancedb/index.ts extensions/memory-lancedb/index.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.
```

```console
$ node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/memory-lancedb/index.ts extensions/memory-lancedb/index.test.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
```

```console
$ git diff --check
# no output
```

Fixes #58139

target.title: Filter assistant process chatter from Dreaming session corpus
target.body:
Fixes #80664. Adds a conservative Dreaming session-corpus hygiene gate that rejects obvious assistant internal process chatter (e.g. `Assistant: Need commit PR`, `Assistant: Now inspect`, `Assistant: Oops ... poll`) before snippets enter session-corpus recall and Light/REM/Deep candidate surfaces, while preserving explicit durable signals such as Arthur/user decisions, verified/PASS artifacts, PR numbers, and commit SHAs.

Validation: added focused tests in `dreaming-phases.test.ts`. Local targeted vitest could not run in the original clone because Corepack/pnpm reported `spawnSync pnpm ENOENT` during dependency setup, so tests are included for CI. After that, this branch was also exercised with the real Dreaming light-session ingestion path against a temporary redacted OpenClaw workspace; proof below.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Dreaming session-corpus ingestion should reject obvious assistant internal process chatter before it can enter session-corpus recall / Light / REM / Deep candidate surfaces, while preserving durable decision and verification signals.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw branch `memory-dreaming-session-corpus-filter` at commit `55b333c6b604df07016a050cb67d3342f8d961ef`, run on a Linux OpenClaw workspace clone with Node `v22.22.0`. The run created a temporary real OpenClaw workspace under `/tmp`, wrote a redacted session transcript under that workspace's `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, then invoked the branch's Dreaming light-session ingestion hook.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
cd /root/.openclaw/workspace/tmp/pr80666-proof/openclaw
git checkout pr-80666
git rev-parse HEAD  # 55b333c6b604df07016a050cb67d3342f8d961ef
node --import tsx tmp/pr80666-real-proof.ts | tee /root/.openclaw/workspace/tmp/pr80666-proof/proof-output.txt
```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture / copied live output, paths redacted):

```text
PR #80666 Dreaming session-corpus real-workspace proof
repo_head=/root/.openclaw/workspace/tmp/pr80666-proof/openclaw
workspace=/tmp/openclaw-pr80666-real-workspace-REDACTED
state_dir=/tmp/openclaw-pr80666-real-workspace-REDACTED/.state
session_transcript=/tmp/openclaw-pr80666-real-workspace-REDACTED/.state/agents/main/sessions/real-pr80666-redacted-session.jsonl
hook_result={"handled":true,"reason":"memory-core: light dreaming processed"}
corpus_path=/tmp/openclaw-pr80666-real-workspace-REDACTED/memory/.dreams/session-corpus/2026-05-12.txt
rejected:PASS_ABSENT:Assistant: Need commit PR.
rejected:PASS_ABSENT:Assistant: Now inspect.
rejected:PASS_ABSENT:Assistant: Oops worktree maybe not created yet due first command still running. poll.
preserved:PASS_PRESENT:Arthur confirmed PR #80666 accepted; verification PASS; commit c33055e8e2ddc12a3ccdac28cfcf0ad5e1be5940.
preserved:PASS_PRESENT:Assistant: The durable decision is recorded with PR #80666 and PASS evidence.
--- redacted corpus excerpt ---
[main/sessions/main/real-pr80666-redacted-session.jsonl#L4] User: Arthur confirmed PR #80666 accepted; verification PASS; commit c33055e8e2ddc12a3ccdac28cfcf0ad5e1be5940.
[main/sessions/main/real-pr80666-redacted-session.jsonl#L5] Assistant: Assistant: The durable decision is recorded with PR #80666 and PASS evidence.
```

- Observed result after fix: The generated `memory/.dreams/session-corpus/2026-05-12.txt` omitted all three assistant process-chatter samples (`Need commit PR`, `Now inspect`, and `Oops ... poll`) and retained the durable `Arthur confirmed PR #80666 ... PASS ... commit c33055e8...` signal plus an assistant-authored durable PR/PASS line. This shows the new ingestion-boundary filter runs before the session-corpus artifact is written, while durable PR / PASS / commit evidence is still available to later Dreaming candidate surfaces.

target.title: docs: clarify backend client behavior with gateway.auth.mode 'none'
target.body:
## Summary

This PR clarifies an important behavior of `gateway.auth.mode: "none"` that is not currently documented.

## Problem

When `gateway.auth.mode` is set to `"none"`, the gateway does not generate or provide a shared authentication token. This causes backend clients (such as embedded agent tools that connect via WebSocket to invoke gateway tools like `sessions_list`, `cron`, or `sessions_spawn`) to fail with `device identity required` errors.

## Evidence

From Gateway logs:

```
# Gateway startup claims all connections are unauthenticated
[gateway] auth mode=none explicitly configured; all gateway connections are unauthenticated.

# But backend clients still fail
gateway connect failed: GatewayClientRequestError: device identity required
[tools] sessions_list failed: gateway closed (1008): device identity required
```

## Solution

Use `gateway.auth.mode: "token"` instead of `"none"` when backend clients need to connect to the gateway.

## Changes

Added a note to the `gateway.auth.mode` documentation in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` to clarify:

1. When set to `none`, the gateway does not generate a shared token
2. Backend clients will fail with `device identity required` errors
3. Recommend using `gateway.auth.mode: "token"` for backend client compatibility

## Testing

This behavior was observed and verified through actual testing:

| `gateway.auth.mode` | Backend Client Behavior | Result |
|---------------------|------------------------|--------|
| `none` | No shared token available | ❌ `device identity required` |
| `token` | Shared token available | ✅ Normal connection |

## Related

This PR documents observed behavior that was discovered through troubleshooting, as discussed in the OpenClaw community.

target.title: fix(media): warn when MEDIA: token is skipped inside fenced code block (#41966)
target.body:
## Problem

When an LLM wraps a `MEDIA:` token inside a triple-backtick code fence, `splitMediaFromOutput()` correctly skips extraction (by design — code examples shouldn't trigger delivery). However, this creates a **silent failure**: the user sees the raw `MEDIA:` path as plain text and receives no media, with no indication of what went wrong.

As reported in #41966, LLMs like GLM-4.7-Flash, Claude, and GPT-4 regularly wrap file paths in code fences, especially when the default `TOOLS.md` template shows `MEDIA:` inside a code block as an example.

## Fix

Emit a `logWarn()` when a `MEDIA:` line is found **inside** a fenced code block. This is a **warn-only** change:

- ✅ No behavior change to extraction logic
- ✅ No new config flags  
- ✅ No changes to `mediaUrls` output
- ✅ Visible text output unchanged (fenced `MEDIA:` lines still render as text)

Warning message shown in logs:
```
media: MEDIA: token skipped — it is inside a fenced code block and will not be delivered. Remove the surrounding backticks/fence to send this as media.
```

## Tests

Three new test cases under `"MEDIA token inside fenced code block emits a warning (#41966)"`:

1. **Warns** on a triple-backtick fenced `MEDIA:` line
2. **Does NOT warn** for `MEDIA:` tokens outside fences (no regression)  
3. **Warns once per fenced MEDIA line**; both lines still appear as visible text (contract unchanged)

TypeScript: clean (`tsc --noEmit` passes).

## Changelog

```
[fix] Emit logWarn when MEDIA: token is silently skipped inside a fenced code block (#41966)
```

## Related

Follows up on my [analysis comment](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/41966#issuecomment-4416096864) proposing Option B (warn-only) as the safe minimal fix before any potential Option A (extract-from-fences) config flag.

---

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** When an LLM wraps a `MEDIA:` token inside a triple-backtick fenced code block, `splitMediaFromOutput()` silently skips the token (by design — code examples must not trigger media delivery) but emits no log signal, so the user sees a raw `MEDIA:` path in chat and has no clue why nothing was delivered. This PR (closes #41966) adds a `logWarn()` so the silent skip becomes diagnosable, with no behavior change to extraction or visible output.

**Real environment tested:**
- Date: 2026-05-27T21:13:03Z
- Host: Darwin 25.4.0 arm64 (macOS, Apple Silicon)
- Node: v25.5.0
- Repo: `fix/41966-media-token-warn-in-fence` @ `5102c3ca43` (Bartok9/openclaw fork)

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
git checkout fix/41966-media-token-warn-in-fence
CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --prefer-offline
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.media.config.ts \
  src/media/parse.test.ts \
  -t "MEDIA token inside fenced code block" \
  --reporter=verbose
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/bartokmoltbot/clawd/openclaw

 ✓  media  src/media/parse.test.ts > splitMediaFromOutput > MEDIA token inside fenced code block emits a warning (#41966) > warns when a MEDIA: token appears inside a triple-backtick fence 23ms
 ✓  media  src/media/parse.test.ts > splitMediaFromOutput > MEDIA token inside fenced code block emits a warning (#41966) > does not warn when a MEDIA: token appears outside a fence 1ms
 ✓  media  src/media/parse.test.ts > splitMediaFromOutput > MEDIA token inside fenced code block emits a warning (#41966) > warns once per fenced MEDIA line and still keeps the line as visible text 0ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed | 57 skipped (60)
   Start at  17:12:52
   Duration  200ms (transform 80ms, setup 80ms, import 18ms, tests 26ms, environment 0ms)
```

Full file run (all 60 cases including the 3 new ones) also passes — 1 file passed, 60/60 tests green at 17:12:43 in 451ms (transform 364ms, setup 233ms, import 50ms, tests 73ms). Confirms no regression in the other `splitMediaFromOutput` behaviors that share `parse.ts`.

**Observed result after fix:**

The three new test cases under `"MEDIA token inside fenced code block emits a warning (#41966)"` exercise the actual `splitMediaFromOutput()` function (no mocks of the unit under test; only `logWarn` is spied on). Case 1 asserts `logWarn` is called with the `"media: MEDIA: token skipped — it is inside a fenced code block…"` message when `MEDIA:` appears between triple backticks. Case 2 asserts `logWarn` is **not** called when `MEDIA:` appears outside any fence (no regression in the happy path). Case 3 asserts each fenced `MEDIA:` line emits exactly one warn, and that the `mediaUrls`/visible-text contract is unchanged (the fenced line still renders as text, no media is lifted). Before this patch the function would skip silently with no log signal; after the patch the warn is observable.

**What was not tested:**

- End-to-end Telegram/Slack rendering of the warn (this is a `logWarn()` change; the test is at the function boundary)
- Localization of the warn string (the string is in English; matches existing `logWarn` conventions in `parse.ts`)
- Behavior with non-triple-backtick fences (4-backtick, tilde, indented code) — out of scope per the issue (`#41966` is specifically about triple-backtick wrapping by LLMs)


---

## Update 2026-06-13 — rebased onto current main

Rebased onto `upstream/main` @ `340c2456bb` to clear the conflict flagged by @RomneyDa. Single commit `5366ecb8` (Bartok9 authorship preserved). Conflicts were trivial (a comment line + the test-file vitest import line, both since adjusted on main); the actual warn logic applied cleanly to the current `splitMediaFromOutput()`.

**Re-verified on the rebased branch (real output):**

`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/media/parse.test.ts` → **60/60 passed**. `oxlint` on the two touched files → **0 warnings, 0 errors**.

Direct function-level repro (no test harness, real `logWarn` output to stderr):

```text
=== CASE 1: MEDIA token INSIDE a fenced code block ===
[media] token skipped — it is inside a fenced code block and will not be delivered. Remove the surrounding backticks/fence to send this as media.
mediaUrls: undefined
kept as visible text: true

=== CASE 2: MEDIA token OUTSIDE a fence (control) ===
mediaUrls: [ 'https://example.com/image.png' ]
```

Confirms the warn fires only for the fenced token, the fenced token is still kept as visible text (not delivered), and the out-of-fence control path is unchanged (extracted to `mediaUrls`, no warn).


---

## Update 2026-06-13 (2) — fixed a real `build-artifacts` regression in this PR

The first rebase pushed surfaced a red `build-artifacts` / "Build dist" check. I traced it to a **genuine regression introduced by the original approach in this PR**, not a main-wide flake:

- `src/media/parse.ts` is bundled into the **browser UI** (`ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.ts` imports `splitMediaFromOutput`).
- The original patch added `import { logWarn } from "../logger.js"` directly in `parse.ts`. That drags the Node-only logger chain (`logger → logging/logger → infra/regular-file → fs-safe-defaults → @openclaw/fs-safe`) into the browser bundle, which can't resolve the `@openclaw/fs-safe/*` extension subpaths → `UNLOADABLE_DEPENDENCY ... No such file or directory`.

**Proof it was ours (local `node scripts/ui.js build`):**

| `parse.ts` variant | UI build |
|---|---|
| with `import { logWarn }` (original) | ❌ `UNLOADABLE_DEPENDENCY` fs-safe, 972 modules |
| clean main (no logger import) | ✅ built in ~1s, 918 modules |

**Fix:** keep `parse.ts` browser-pure via dependency injection. Added an optional `onFencedMediaTokenSkipped(line)` callback to `SplitMediaFromOutputOptions`; the Node-side caller `parseReplyDirectives()` (`src/auto-reply/reply/reply-directives.ts`) injects the real `logWarn`. No Node-only import in `parse.ts` anymore.

**Re-verified (`08170177b1`):**
- `node scripts/ui.js build` → ✅ built, 918 modules (matches clean main)
- `src/media/parse.test.ts` → 61/61 passing
- `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-state.test.ts` → 40/40 passing
- `oxlint` on the 3 touched files → 0 warnings / 0 errors
- End-to-end repro through `parseReplyDirectives()` (real Node path):

```text
=== CASE 1: fenced MEDIA via parseReplyDirectives (node path) ===
[media] token skipped — it is inside a fenced code block and will not be delivered. Remove the surrounding backticks/fence to send this as media.
mediaUrls: undefined | kept as text: true

=== CASE 2: unfenced MEDIA (control) ===
mediaUrls: [ 'https://example.com/image.png' ]
```

target.title: feat(tools/write): add append mode for agent writes
target.body:
## Summary

Revives and rebases the closed append-mode work from #75549 for the canonical data-loss issue #40001.

The `write` tool currently overwrites existing files. OpenClaw has an append-only wrapper for memory-flush runs, but ordinary agent turns, cron skills, and monitor-and-react workflows do not have a first-class append path. This PR adds a backwards-compatible optional `append: true` parameter to `write` so agents can append without synthesizing a read-concatenate-overwrite sequence.

When `append` is omitted or false, existing overwrite behavior is unchanged.

## What changed

- Adds optional `append?: boolean` handling for host/workspace and sandbox write operations.
- Preserves existing workspace/root guard behavior.
- Serializes concurrent appends through the existing file mutation queue.
- Preserves validation behavior for blank paths and invalid content.
- Surfaces sandbox I/O failures except for missing-file cases used to create-on-append.
- Honors aborted append calls before entering the mutation queue.
- Adds focused regression coverage for append-create, append-accumulate, append-false-overwrites, validation, and sandbox/workspace behavior.

## Validation

Run in an isolated checkout on current `origin/main`:

- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.read.host-tilde-expansion.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.params.test.ts`
  - passed: `10 files / 170 tests`
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm lint:core`
  - passed: `0 warnings / 0 errors`

## Notes

This PR intentionally keeps the API small and backwards-compatible. It does not remove or relax the existing memory-flush append-only wrapper; it gives ordinary `write` callers an explicit append path instead of relying on prompt discipline or destructive read/concatenate/write patterns.

Fixes #40001.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** The agent write tool supports `append: true` without overwriting the existing file, while still using the workspace-root guard for workspace-only host writes.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux using the actual `createHostWorkspaceWriteTool` implementation against a temporary workspace on the real filesystem.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx` terminal smoke that created the host workspace write tool, wrote `first line`, reran the same write tool with `append: true` for `second line`, then read the resulting file.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from the smoke:

  ```text
  workspace: $TMPDIR/openclaw-write-append-proof-.../workspace
  tool result: append:true preserved existing content and added the second line
  file content:
  first line
  second line
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The final file retained the original content and appended the new content in order; the append path did not truncate or replace the file.
- **What was not tested:** No live Gateway session was exercised; validation used the real tool implementation in an isolated local checkout plus targeted regression/type checks.

## Summary

Fixes the Windows `openclaw doctor` shell-completion cache path observed in the maintainer workshop: doctor was spawning `openclaw completion --write-state` without the plugin-command skip guard, so completion cache generation could load plugin CLI commands and trigger bundled runtime dependency work during doctor.

The update path already sets `OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_SKIP_PLUGIN_COMMANDS=1` for this same cache write. This applies the same guard to doctor-triggered completion cache writes.

- `pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts`

target.title: fix(memory): prevent cross-agent dreaming contamination (Bug #65374)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a cross-agent identity contamination vector in the memory dreaming pipeline. When multiple agents share a workspace, `resolveSessionAgentsForWorkspace()` returns ALL agent IDs with zero isolation, allowing one agent's dreaming sweep to ingest another agent's sessions.

**Status: Implementation complete. All layers committed and tested. Ready for upstream review.**

## Root Cause

`resolveMemoryDreamingWorkspaces()` groups agents by workspace with no isolation boundary. The `currentAgentId` exists in `PluginHookAgentContext` but is never threaded into the dreaming pipeline, so shared-workspace configs silently cross-contaminate session data.

## Changes (6 commits)

### Layer 1: Workspace type hardening (bd829020)
- Add `shared: boolean` flag to `MemoryDreamingWorkspace` type
- Log warnings when shared workspaces are detected

### Layer 2a: Agent identity threading (bd829020)
- Thread `currentAgentId` from `ctx.agentId` through the full dreaming pipeline

### Layer 2b: Fail-closed isolation (bd829020, aad87c3b)
- When workspace is shared AND `currentAgentId` is undefined, empty, or whitespace-only → skip dreaming entirely
- Whitespace-only bypass closed per security audit (Ghost)

### Layer 2c: Per-agent corpus files (38f10153)
- Shared workspaces write to `session-corpus/{agentId}/{day}.txt` instead of shared file
- Read-path isolation via `filterRecallEntriesForAgentIsolation()`

### Layer 3: Provenance sidecar (38f10153, fba00d60)
- Separate `memory/.dreams/provenance.json` with SHA-256 content hashes
- Entries include: id, agentId, sessionKey, promotedAt, score, contentHash, sourcePath, sourceLineRange
- sessionKey threaded from dreaming context through to provenance write

### Tests + Security Audit
- 17 tests covering all layers + adversarial edge cases
- 5-target security audit by Ghost, 1 actionable finding (whitespace bypass) fixed

## Files Changed

- `src/memory-host-sdk/dreaming.ts` — type definition + shared flag + tests
- `src/memory-host-sdk/dreaming.test.ts` — 2 shared flag tests  
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts` — warnings, currentAgentId threading, fail-closed logic, whitespace trim
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts` — phase-level isolation tests
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.ts` — shared workspace warning, currentAgentId + sessionKey passthrough
- `extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts` — sessionKey in options + provenance entry, wired to sidecar
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-isolation.test.ts` — 17 isolation + adversarial tests

target.title: fix(qqbot): gate updateLastRoute to group/guild turns only
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem**: PR #94785 added `updateLastRoute` to persist QQ Bot group delivery routes for announce target resolution, but the guard condition `event.type !== "c2c"` does not exclude `dm` (direct message) events. QQ Bot has three non-group event types (`c2c`, `dm`, `guild` channel message), and `DIRECT_MESSAGE_CREATE` normalizes to `event.type === "dm"` — which satisfies `event.type !== "c2c"` and would overwrite group delivery metadata in the shared session.

**Solution**: Replace the `event.type !== "c2c"` guard with `inbound.isGroupChat`, which is already computed by `runAccessStage` as `event.type === "guild" || event.type === "group"`. This correctly gates route persistence to group and guild turns only, matching the established pattern used by other channels (Discord checks `isDirectMessage`, iMessage checks `!decision.isGroup`, LINE checks `!params.source.isGroup`).

**What changed**: Three files, 169 additions, 3 deletions.
- `config-shared.ts`: Added `preferSessionLookupForAnnounceTarget: true` to `qqbotMeta`
- `outbound-dispatch.ts`: Replaced `event.type !== "c2c"` with `inbound.isGroupChat` in the `updateLastRoute` spread conditional within the record block
- `outbound-dispatch.test.ts`: Added 4 focused tests (group/guild writes asserted, c2c/dm omissions asserted)

**What did NOT change**: No changes to announce message creation, session store, or other dispatch paths. No changes to the `updateLastRoute` payload shape or the record block structure — only the guard condition changed.

Fixes #86387

---

## Linked context

- **Issue**: #86387 — QQ Bot group route persistence and sessions_send announce misrouting
- **Supersedes**: #94785 — Original PR that used `event.type !== "c2c"` guard, now superceded by this fix
- **Root cause cluster**: `DIRECT_MESSAGE_CREATE` → `event.type === "dm"` passes `event.type !== "c2c"` guard (event-dispatcher.ts:101, access-stage.ts:25)
- **In scope**: Gate `updateLastRoute` to group/guild only; add focused route regression tests
- **Out of scope**: Multi-session concurrency stress testing, Gateway restart cycle persistence

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: QQ Bot group/guild messages persist `updateLastRoute` into session deliveryContext; c2c/dm messages do not, preserving group delivery metadata from DM route overwrite.

**Real environment tested**: Linux 6.8.0-124-generic / Node.js 25.9.0 / OpenClaw via `pnpm dev gateway` (production config, incremental build ~31s) / QQ Bot appId 1903562876 connected to production QQ Bot WebSocket gateway wss://api.sgroup.qq.com/websocket / log capture from /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-06-30.log

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
cd openclaw && pnpm dev gateway
```
Send a c2c message to the QQ Bot from a QQ client, then send a group message in a QQ group the bot belongs to. Monitor logs:
```bash
tail -f /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-06-30.log
```

**After-fix evidence**: ```
2026-06-30T20:17:31.661+08:00 [channels/qqbot] Processing message from <redacted>: 你好 type="c2c"
2026-06-30T20:17:31.924+08:00 [channels/qqbot] [route-persistence] inbound event.type=c2c isGroupChat=false -> omitting updateLastRoute
2026-06-30T20:18:02.415+08:00 [channels/qqbot] Processing message from <redacted>: 你好 type="group"
2026-06-30T20:18:02.422+08:00 [channels/qqbot] [route-persistence] inbound event.type=group isGroupChat=true -> applying updateLastRoute
```

**Observed result after the fix**: Real QQ Bot runtime confirms both paths. Group messages: `isGroupChat=true` → `updateLastRoute` persisted into the turn's `record` object with sessionKey, channel (`qqbot`), `to` (qualifiedTarget), and `accountId`. C2c messages: `isGroupChat=false` → `updateLastRoute` omitted from the record, leaving session deliveryContext unchanged. The `[route-persistence]` diagnostic log was added temporarily for proof collection and has been removed from the final committed source after capturing evidence.

**What was not tested**: Multiple concurrent group conversations interleaving rapidly; route persistence across Gateway restart cycles; failover when QQ Bot API returns non-200 after updateLastRoute is persisted.

## Tests and validation

### Unit tests

```
npx vitest run extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  27 passed (27)
   Start at  14:14:39
   Duration  8.79s
```

New tests added:
- `route persistence > includes updateLastRoute in record for group inbound` — asserts `record.updateLastRoute` present with correct payload shape, covering sessionKey, channel, to, accountId
- `route persistence > includes updateLastRoute in record for guild inbound` — same shape assertion for guild channel messages (QQ Bot guild channels map to `event.type === "guild"`)
- `route persistence > excludes updateLastRoute from record for c2c inbound` — asserts `record.updateLastRoute` is undefined for c2c events
- `route persistence > excludes updateLastRoute from record for dm inbound` — asserts `record.updateLastRoute` is undefined for dm events (the key fix: `event.type === "dm"` previously passed the old `event.type !== "c2c"` guard)

Test harness modified to expose resolved turn object via optional `onTurn` callback on `makeRuntime`, enabling record inspection without changing dispatch behavior.

### Integration

Not applicable — the change is a single guard condition in the dispatch record pipeline. Real QQ Bot runtime verification (see Real behavior proof above) provides the equivalent of integration coverage across the full gateway dispatch path.

## Risk checklist

- [x] This change is backwards compatible. Existing group/guild behavior unchanged; c2c was already excluded and remains excluded; dm is newly excluded which is the intended fix.
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations. All 23 existing tests pass unchanged; 4 new tests provide focused coverage.
- [x] I have updated relevant documentation. No public API changes; internal guard condition change only.
- [ ] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary — N/A, no breaking changes.

**Merge-risk**: Low — single guard condition change in the `record` block of `resolveTurn`. No structural changes to the dispatch pipeline, session store, or announce message creation. Risk mitigated by 4 new focused unit tests and real QQ Bot runtime verification. Rollback scope: revert one condition in one file.

## Current review state

- [x] Self-review completed
- [ ] At least one maintainer review
- [ ] All CI checks passed

target.title: fix(daemon): prefer bundled node for gateway service
target.body:
## Summary

- Prefer an installed OpenClaw bundled Node runtime from the active state dir when generating gateway service runtime paths.
- Validate discovered `tools/node-v*/bin/node` candidates before using them.
- Fall back to the current/system Node behavior when no supported bundled runtime exists.

## Why

On macOS it is possible for `openclaw gateway install` to capture an arbitrary PATH/current Node such as `~/.local/bin/node`, Homebrew, fnm, or nvm into the LaunchAgent even though OpenClaw has already installed a stable bundled runtime under `~/.openclaw/tools/node-v*/bin/node`. That can make the gateway drift with unrelated user Node changes.

Refs #75250.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
- Gateway service installs should not drift to an unrelated current/PATH Node when OpenClaw already has a supported bundled state-dir Node available.

Real environment tested:
- macOS local OpenClaw v2026.5.5 install with LaunchAgent `ai.openclaw.gateway`.
- Bundled Node present under the OpenClaw state dir: `/Users/tulisy/.openclaw/tools/node-v22.22.0/bin/node`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- Refreshed this branch against current `origin/main` and resolved the runtime-path conflicts.
- Checked the installed LaunchAgent program arguments with `plutil -p "$HOME/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist"`.
- Checked the live gateway process command with `ps -axo command | rg '/Users/tulisy/.openclaw/tools/node-v[0-9.]+/bin/node|openclaw.*gateway'`.

Evidence after fix:
```text
LaunchAgent ProgramArguments[2]: /Users/tulisy/.openclaw/tools/node-v22.22.0/bin/node
Live gateway command starts with: /Users/tulisy/.openclaw/tools/node-v22.22.0/bin/node /Users/tulisy/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/index.js gateway --port 18789
```

Observed result after fix:
- The gateway service and live gateway process use the OpenClaw bundled state-dir Node instead of Homebrew/fnm/nvm/current PATH Node.
- The PR diff is narrow after the latest main refresh: `CHANGELOG.md`, `src/daemon/runtime-paths.ts`, and `src/daemon/runtime-paths.test.ts`.

What was not tested:
- The focused Vitest/oxfmt commands could not be rerun in this lightweight PR worktree after the latest main refresh because this checkout has no local `node_modules`; GitHub exact-head CI is the authoritative validation for this refresh.

## Validation

Previously on this PR branch:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.daemon.config.ts src/daemon/runtime-paths.test.ts` -> passed, 20 tests
- `oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/daemon/runtime-paths.ts src/daemon/runtime-paths.test.ts` -> passed

After refreshing against current `origin/main`:
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` -> passed
- PR diff narrowed back to 3 files after removing unrelated formatting-only changes.

target.title: docs(capabilities): define workspace discovery convention
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2-5 bullets:

- Problem: workspace-local integrations and durable procedures can be documented ad hoc, so agents may miss valid local capability paths before saying a task is unsupported.
- Why it matters: users with bridge queues, taskflow recipes, ACP delegation paths, or local integration wrappers need a consistent way to make those capabilities discoverable across sessions.
- What changed: added a docs-only workspace capability descriptor convention under `capabilities/*.md`, linked it from workspace/bootstrap docs, and documented current bootstrap-extra-files limits.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no runtime auto-discovery, gateway wrapper, daemon, auth layer, session store, or tool policy change.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix

- [ ] Chore/infra

N/A.

N/A.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local checkout

- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Read `docs/concepts/workspace-capabilities.md`.
2. Confirm `docs/concepts/agent-workspace.md` links the optional `capabilities/` workspace folder.
3. Confirm `docs/concepts/system-prompt.md` and `docs/automation/hooks.md` state that descriptors are not part of the fixed bootstrap set by default.

### Expected

- The docs describe a narrow workspace capability descriptor convention without implying runtime permission or gateway behavior changes.

### Actual

- Matches expected.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Commands:

```bash

git diff --check
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/concepts/workspace-capabilities.md docs/concepts/agent-workspace.md docs/concepts/system-prompt.md docs/automation/hooks.md docs/docs.json

```

● - [x] Add FIELD_ADVANCED set in schema.hints.ts to mark technical config fields (auth cooldowns, gateway infra knobs, diagnostics subsections, etc.) as advanced, so the config form can hide
   them by default
  - [x] Add docLink field to ConfigUiHint type for future per-field docs links
  - [x] In config-form.node.ts renderObject, split top-level section fields into basic (visible by default) and advanced (collapsed <details> disclosure) when no search is active; search
  bypasses the split so all matching fields are always visible
  - [x] Add renderHelpBlock helper that renders help text, doc link, and tags together; render the Docs ↗ anchor when hints.docLink is set
  - [x] Add CSS for .cfg-advanced-section disclosure and .cfg-field__doc-link

  ## Summary

  - **Problem:** The config UI shows all settings at the same level — basic toggles sit alongside low-level infra knobs (auth backoff timers, gateway health-check intervals, otel settings),
  making the page feel dense and hard to navigate for non-expert users.
  - **Why it matters:** New users opening the config page see 50+ fields at once with no visual hierarchy; important settings like log level or update channel are buried next to
  rarely-touched tuning parameters.
  - **What changed:** Top-level fields in each config section are now split into "basic" (always visible) and "advanced" (collapsed `<details>` disclosure with a count badge). The split is
  bypassed when a search query is active so all matching fields remain discoverable. The `ConfigUiHint` type gains an optional `docLink` field; when set, a `Docs ↗` anchor is rendered inline
  with the help text.
  - **What did NOT change:** Raw mode, search behavior, the Form/Raw toggle, nested-object collapsibles, tag filtering (`tag:advanced`), and plugin-owned channel hints are all untouched.

  4. Click "Advanced" disclosure → cooldown fields appear
