target.title: feat(feishu): add topicRequireMention for topic thread mention control
target.body:
Summary

  - Problem: requireMention in Feishu groups applies equally to normal group messages and topic thread replies, with no way to separate the two. Users who want to require @mention for new
  conversations but allow free replies inside started threads cannot do so.
  - Why it matters: In busy group chats, requiring @mention on every topic reply is noisy and breaks the natural conversation flow inside a thread.
  - What changed: Added optional topicRequireMention boolean at top-level, per-account, and per-group config levels. When a message has thread_id set (Feishu topic thread),
  topicRequireMention takes precedence over requireMention. Falls back to existing requireMention cascade when unset.
  - What did NOT change: DM behavior, topic session routing (groupSessionScope, topicSessionMode), replyInThread, broadcast dispatch, and all other group access-control logic are unchanged.

  Change Type (select all)

  - Bug fix
  - Feature
  - Refactor required for the fix
  - Docs
  - Security hardening
  - Chore/infra

  Scope (select all touched areas)

  - Gateway / orchestration
  - Skills / tool execution
  - Auth / tokens
  - Memory / storage
  - Integrations
  - API / contracts
  - UI / DX
  - CI/CD / infra

  Linked Issue/PR

  - Closes #76010
  - This PR fixes a bug or regression

  Root Cause (if applicable)

  N/A — this is a new feature, not a bug fix.

  Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

  N/A — new feature, no regression to guard against. New test coverage added instead:

  - Coverage level:
    - Unit test ✓
    - Seam / integration test
    - End-to-end test
    - Existing coverage already sufficient
  - Target test or file: extensions/feishu/src/policy.test.ts, extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts, extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts
  - Scenario the test should lock in: topic thread messages bypass mention gate when topicRequireMention=false; non-topic messages are unaffected by topicRequireMention
  - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: policy resolution is pure and fully unit-testable; bot-level integration test covers the full dispatch path
  - Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
  - If no new test is added, why not: Tests were added (6 unit + 2 integration + 5 schema)

  User-visible / Behavior Changes

  New optional config keys:

  - channels.feishu.topicRequireMention — top-level override for topic thread messages
  - channels.feishu.groups.<chat_id>.topicRequireMention — per-group override

  When unset, behavior is identical to today. No defaults changed.

  Diagram (if applicable)

  Before:
  [group message] -> requireMention check -> respond / drop

  After:
  [group message, thread_id set] -> topicRequireMention (if set) -> respond / drop
                                                 ↓ (fallback)
  [group message, no thread_id]  -> requireMention check          -> respond / drop

  Security Impact (required)

  - New permissions/capabilities? No
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
  - New/changed network calls? No
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? No
  - Data access scope changed? No

  Repro + Verification

  Environment

  - OS: Linux
  - Runtime/container: Node 22
  - Model/provider: any
  - Integration/channel: Feishu (websocket mode)
  - Relevant config (redacted):

  {
    channels: {
      feishu: {
        groupPolicy: "allowlist",
        groups: {
          "oc_xxx": {
            requireMention: true,
            topicRequireMention: false,
          },
        },
      },
    },
  }

  Steps

  1. Configure group with requireMention: true, topicRequireMention: false
  2. Send a normal group message without @mention → bot should not respond
  3. Send a message inside a topic thread without @mention → bot should respond

  Expected

  - Step 2: no response
  - Step 3: bot responds

  Actual

  - Verified via unit and integration tests (712 passed)

  Evidence

  - Failing test/log before + passing after — 6 new unit tests in policy.test.ts, 2 integration tests in bot.test.ts, 5 schema tests in config-schema.test.ts, all passing (712 total)

  Human Verification (required)

  - Verified scenarios: policy resolution cascade (per-group beats top-level, topic beats non-topic, DM short-circuit), schema acceptance/rejection at all three levels, bot dispatch gate with
   and without thread_id
  - Edge cases checked: topicRequireMention absent → full backwards compat; DM with both knobs set → still false; wildcard group entry with topicRequireMention
  - What you did not verify: live Feishu WebSocket delivery (no test credentials available)

  Review Conversations

  - I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
  - I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

  Compatibility / Migration

  - Backward compatible? Yes
  - Config/env changes? Yes — two new optional config keys (topicRequireMention at top-level and per-group); no breaking changes, no migration needed
  - Migration needed? No

  Risks and Mitigations

  - Risk: operator sets topicRequireMention: false globally and accidentally opens the bot to all topic thread messages without realizing requireMention only guards the main chat now
    - Mitigation: documented clearly in docs/channels/feishu.md with an explicit example showing the combined use of both flags

target.title: fix(memory-core): add actionable remediation hints for memory search embedding errors
target.body:
## Summary

Fresh port of #55684 onto current `main` (the source branch was 16k commits behind and unmergeable). Adds actionable remediation hints when `memory_search` or `openclaw memory status` surfaces an embedding-provider failure, so leaked-key, quota, and invalid-auth errors stop printing as raw provider strings without recovery guidance.

Fixes #54912. Supersedes #55684 (closed-stale).

## What changed

- **`extensions/memory-core/src/tools.shared.ts`** — adds `resolveEmbeddingErrorKind` (classifies leaked / quota / invalid_key) and `resolveEmbeddingErrorHint`. `buildMemorySearchUnavailableResult` now prefers the kind-specific hint over the previous generic `action` text.
- **`extensions/memory-core/src/cli.runtime.ts`** — adds `resolveEmbeddingErrorRemediation` with provider-specific env-var hints (`GEMINI_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `VOYAGE_API_KEY`) and emits a `Fix` line in `runMemoryStatus` after `Embeddings error`.
- **Tests** — `tools.test.ts` and `tools.citations.test.ts` updated for the new quota action text; new tests for leaked-key and invalid-key paths.
- **`CHANGELOG.md`** — single-line entry under Unreleased › Fixes (per repo policy and Codex P3 review on #55684).

## Codex review notes addressed (from #55684)

- ✅ Branch is now on current `main` (no longer 16k commits diverged).
- ✅ CHANGELOG entry added (the P3 finding from the prior Codex pass).
- ✅ Focused tests for leaked-key, quota, and invalid-key classification added in `tools.test.ts`.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 …` — clean.
- [x] `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts` — 20/20 pass (2 new tests included).

target.title: fix(memory): improve QMD recall for channel queries
target.body:
## Summary

- allow QMD recall for Discord channel sessions in the default scope
- retry empty/noisy QMD search queries once with a tighter lexical query built from priority memory anchors
- report scope-denied QMD searches in debug output instead of making them indistinguishable from true zero-hit searches

## Why

Gary's live Samantha tests in Discord showed `memory_search` using QMD but returning zero hits for a noisy memory query. Local log inspection showed the first failure mode was QMD scope denial for Discord channel sessions. After allowing channel sessions, the intentionally noisy query could reach QMD, but needed a conservative lexical retry to recover useful memory hits rather than stopping at an empty result.

This stays separate from #75761, which handles active-memory timeout behavior.

## Validation

- `pnpm test packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-scope.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- live Samantha Discord test: the noisy query `thread memory retrieval active-memory QMD semantic memory search Discord test` returned 4 QMD hits with `fallback: relaxed-lexical-zero-hit`, and Samantha was able to follow with `memory_get`

target.title: Gateway: guard session-run cancel requester in minimal tests
target.body:
## Summary
- Rebased this replacement lane onto latest `origin/main` and kept the minimal-test-gateway requester guard from the prior patch.
- Bounded sticky session-run cancel replay state with a 10 minute TTL plus a 1,000-entry cap, while preserving late-handler replay semantics.
- Added a production cleanup path: registered chat/agent run teardown now clears the matching sticky cancel target after the owning run finishes/aborts.
- Documented `openclaw/plugin-sdk/session-run-cancel-runtime` in `docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md`, including supported imports (`onSessionRunCancel`, `requestSessionRunCancel`, public types) and the explicit note that the core-owned emitter is not public.
- Added the required `CHANGELOG.md` Unreleased entry for the Plugin SDK import path and Gateway cancel behavior.

## Integration note
- #75371 remains the clean replacement lane for #68624. It no longer carries the earlier stacked `sessions_send` scope from #68622.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Plugin-owned delegated tasks can use `openclaw/plugin-sdk/session-run-cancel-runtime` to request cancellation of the owning Gateway session run; Gateway aborts the run, emits the core-owned cancel to plugin observers, preserves sticky replay briefly for late registration, and clears sticky replay during run cleanup. The core-owned emitter remains unavailable through the public plugin SDK subpath.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `f9343ffcda` on Node `v22.22.2` in the repository worktree, using the real in-process Gateway chat-abort runtime (`registerChatAbortController` + `wireSessionRunCancelRequester`) and the plugin-facing session-run-cancel runtime import path. No external network sends or secrets were used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a one-off local runtime proof script with `node --import tsx <proof-script>.mts`. The script registered a real Gateway chat abort controller, wired `wireSessionRunCancelRequester(...)`, imported `onSessionRunCancel` and `requestSessionRunCancel` through the plugin SDK runtime source path, requested cancel for `{ kind:"session_run", sessionKey:"agent:proof:main", runId:"proof-run-1" }`, then checked the Gateway abort signal, plugin observer callback, sticky replay before cleanup, and cleanup state after `active.cleanup()`.

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the local proof command:

```json
{
  "proof": "local gateway chat-abort runtime + public plugin SDK session-run-cancel-runtime",
  "publicPluginImport": "openclaw/plugin-sdk/session-run-cancel-runtime",
  "coreEmitterPublic": false,
  "requestResult": { "requested": true, "aborted": true },
  "abortSignalAborted": true,
  "pluginObservedCancel": {
    "target": { "kind": "session_run", "sessionKey": "agent:proof:main", "runId": "proof-run-1" },
    "reason": { "source": "chat-abort", "message": "real runtime proof" }
  },
  "stickyReplayBeforeRunCleanup": { "source": "chat-abort", "message": "real runtime proof" },
  "stickyReplayAfterRunCleanupCount": 0,
  "terminalStateAfterRunCleanup": false,
  "abortedBroadcast": { "runId": "proof-run-1", "sessionKey": "agent:proof:main", "seq": 5, "state": "aborted", "stopReason": "real runtime proof" },
  "nodeSessionEventCount": 1,
  "boundedTerminalMaxEntries": 1000,
  "boundedTerminalTtlMs": 600000
}
```

Observed result after fix: The plugin-side cancel request returned `{ requested:true, aborted:true }`; the Gateway AbortController signal was aborted; the plugin observer saw exactly one `{ source:"chat-abort" }` cancellation; late registration replayed the sticky cancel before run cleanup; run cleanup removed sticky replay (`stickyReplayAfterRunCleanupCount:0`, `terminalStateAfterRunCleanup:false`); and the Gateway emitted an aborted chat payload with the requested stop reason.

What was not tested: External channel delivery, remote production Gateway deployment, and third-party plugin package publishing were not exercised in this local proof.

## Verification
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/sessions/session-run-cancel.test.ts src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/session-run-cancel-runtime.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-early.test.ts` → pass (6 files, 52 tests across configured projects)
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts --testNamePattern "smoke: supports abort and idempotent completion"` → pass
- `pnpm run plugin-sdk:api:gen && pnpm run plugin-sdk:api:check` → pass (`OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256`)
- `pnpm run lint:plugins:plugin-sdk-subpaths-exported` → pass
- `pnpm run format:docs:check` → pass
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md` → pass
- `pnpm run lint:core` → pass
- `pnpm run build` → pass

target.title: fix(ui): improve Thai locale copy
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the new Thai Control UI locale still had high-visible English fallback copy and a few unnatural Thai labels around common controls, the agent detail surface, files, channels, cron panels, and the language picker.
- Why it matters: Thai users see these strings immediately after selecting Thai, and the docs/UI translation pipeline needs one shared terminology style.
- What changed: added Thai UI/docs glossary terms, updated Thai translation memory, regenerated `ui/src/i18n/locales/th.ts`, added a Thai copy regression test, and fixed the i18n generator process decoder so formatted multibyte locale output does not get replacement characters when stdout chunks split UTF-8 bytes.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no locale registry changes, no supported-locale changes, no docs generated-output edits under `docs/th/**`, no Mintlify/navigation behavior changes, and no public API or runtime contract changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #70510
- Related #3460
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Thai was recently enabled in the Control UI picker, but the Thai translation memory still carried English fallbacks and weaker terminology for high-visible agent/file/channel labels. While regenerating, the formatter subprocess output path could also decode UTF-8 chunks with `String(chunk)`, which can corrupt Thai text if a multibyte character is split across chunks.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing i18n tests checked structural alignment and non-English fallback health, but did not lock specific Thai high-visible labels or replacement-character regressions.
- Contributing context (if known): #70510 tracks broader Thai docs feedback, so this PR intentionally updates glossary sources and Control UI copy without editing generated docs output.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Thai common, language picker, agent, file preview, and cron delivery copy stays localized and does not contain the prior replacement-character corruption.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these keys are generated output consumed directly by the Control UI, so a locale-level unit assertion catches both TM drift and generator corruption.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): structural locale alignment already existed in the same file, but it did not assert Thai wording quality.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Thai Control UI copy is more natural for high-visible labels such as Agent, Workspace, Tools, Channel, Default, Preview, language names, and file preview controls. Future Thai docs/UI translation runs also get the updated mixed technical glossary.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[select Thai] -> [agent/files/channels UI] -> [mixed English fallbacks and awkward labels]

After:
[select Thai] -> [agent/files/channels UI] -> [Thai localized labels using shared glossary terms]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 26.4.1
- Runtime/container: Node v24.13.0, pnpm 10.33.2
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI i18n
- Relevant config (redacted): no translation provider configured; existing TM cache used

### Steps

1. Regenerate Thai Control UI locale from source translation memory.
2. Run i18n drift, targeted locale tests, docs glossary checks, formatter checks, and changed gate.
3. Inspect the generated Thai bundle for replacement-character corruption.

### Expected

- Thai locale bundle stays generator-clean, high-visible Thai labels are localized, and glossary source files stay valid.

### Actual

- Expected behavior confirmed locally.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Validation run:

```text
node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts sync --locale th --write
pnpm ui:i18n:check
pnpm test ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts src/i18n/registry.test.ts
pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts ui/src/i18n/locales/th.ts
git diff --check
OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=1 OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled pnpm check:changed
```

Notes:

- `pnpm test ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts src/i18n/registry.test.ts` passed 2 Vitest shards: 81 UI files / 786 UI tests and 1 unit-fast file / 3 tests.
- `pnpm check:changed` selected core, coreTests, docs, and tooling lanes and completed successfully.
- Blacksmith Testbox was not run because the local `blacksmith` CLI is not installed. The unpinned remote installer was not executed; the local changed gate above was used as the fallback.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: regenerated Thai locale from TM, verified `ui:i18n:check` clean for all locales, verified Thai high-visible copy assertions, verified docs Thai glossary source check, verified no replacement characters remain in the Thai locale/TM, and verified local changed gate.
- Edge cases checked: UTF-8 multibyte formatter output from the i18n generator, language picker Thai/English labels, agent workspace/file preview labels, and cron delivery summary label.
- What you did **not** verify: browser screenshot of the Control UI and Blacksmith Testbox, because the Blacksmith CLI was unavailable locally.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Thai wording preferences can be subjective.
  - Mitigation: kept the scope to high-visible UI labels and encoded the mixed technical style in both UI and docs glossaries so future translation passes stay consistent.
- Risk: generator script change touches shared tooling.
  - Mitigation: narrowed the change to UTF-8 stream decoding for subprocess output and covered the observed Thai replacement-character failure with a locale test plus `ui:i18n:check`.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Thai Control UI high-visible labels now load from the regenerated Thai locale bundle with localized agent, workspace, channel, file-preview, and language picker copy instead of the prior English/awkward fallback wording.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS 26.4.1 with Node v24.13.0 and pnpm 10.33.2, using the generated `ui/src/i18n/locales/th.ts` bundle after the patch.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Loaded the generated Thai Control UI locale bundle with Node/tsx and printed the labels a Thai user would see in the agent/file/language picker surfaces.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from a live `node` locale import in this OpenClaw checkout:

```text
node --import tsx -e "import { th } from \"./ui/src/i18n/locales/th.ts\"; const proof = { commonDefault: th.agents.default, agentTabsTools: th.agents.tabs.tools, agentChannelsTitle: th.agents.channels.title, agentWorkspace: th.agents.context.workspace, filePreview: th.agents.files.preview, fileEmptyDraft: th.agents.files.emptyDraft, thaiLanguageLabel: th.languages.th }; console.log(JSON.stringify(proof, null, 2));"
{
  "commonDefault": "ค่าเริ่มต้น",
  "agentTabsTools": "เครื่องมือ",
  "agentChannelsTitle": "ช่องทาง",
  "agentWorkspace": "เวิร์กสเปซ",
  "filePreview": "ดูตัวอย่าง",
  "fileEmptyDraft": "ร่างว่าง",
  "thaiLanguageLabel": "ไทย"
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The generated Thai bundle returns localized Thai strings for the visible Agent/Tools/Channel/Workspace/File Preview/Thai language labels, with no replacement-character corruption in the sampled output.
- **What was not tested**: Browser screenshot of the Control UI and Blacksmith Testbox, because the local `blacksmith` and `crabbox` CLIs are not installed in this environment.

target.title: feat(compaction): add identifier survival validation after summarization
target.body:
Fixes #90010
Fixes #79588

## Summary

Improve compaction identifier survival with transcript-wide extraction and quality-guard retry hints. Fixes two security/correctness issues found during review:

**Fix 1 — Sanitize string-encoded JSON arguments before extraction**: `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers` now parses string-encoded tool arguments as JSON and passes the parsed object through `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload` before identifier scanning. Previously, string arguments (e.g. OpenAI's JSON-stringified format) were appended raw, allowing credential values like `{"token":"deadbeef12345678"}` to feed the hex identifier extractor and leak into `## Exact identifiers`.

**Fix 2 — Fail strict compaction when cap recovery cannot fit**: When strict mode detects that final capping removed required identifiers and the recovery block would exceed `MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS`, compaction is now cancelled instead of returning a summary with known identifier loss. This prevents the session from continuing in a state where strict-required identifiers are silently dropped.

## Changed files

- `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard-quality.ts` — Parse string args as JSON, sanitize via `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload` before appending; non-JSON strings still pass through
- `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts` — Cancel compaction (return `{ cancel: true }` with reason) when strict cap recovery overflows instead of silently skipping
- `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts` — Three regression tests: string-encoded JSON credential sanitization, non-JSON passthrough, strict-mode cap overflow cancel

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Two security/correctness issues in compaction identifier survival. (1) `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers` appended string-encoded tool arguments raw, allowing credential values like `{"token":"deadbeef12345678"}` to feed the hex identifier extractor and leak into `## Exact identifiers`. (2) When strict mode detected that final capping removed required identifiers and the recovery block would exceed `MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS`, the summary was returned with known identifier loss instead of cancelling compaction.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5 arm64, Node v22.19.0, pnpm v11.2.2. Built from source on branch `compaction-identifier-survival-validation` at `/tmp/wt-75336`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

Step 1. Create proof script exercising production `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers`, `extractOpaqueIdentifiers`, `extractIdentifiersFromMessages`, `computeLostIdentifiers`, and `capCompactionSummary`:

```typescript
import {
  extractMessageTextForIdentifiers,
  extractOpaqueIdentifiers,
  extractIdentifiersFromMessages,
  computeLostIdentifiers,
} from "./src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard-quality.js";
import { testing } from "./src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.js";
const { capCompactionSummary, MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS } = testing;
```

Step 2. Validate Fix 1, string-encoded JSON args with credential `token` field:

```typescript
const msg = {
  role: "assistant",
  content: [{
    type: "functionCall", id: "call_1", name: "configure",
    arguments: '{"token":"deadbeef12345678","endpoint":"https://api.acme.io/v2"}',
  }],
};
const text = extractMessageTextForIdentifiers(msg);
assert(!text.includes("deadbeef12345678"));    // credential redacted
assert(text.includes("https://api.acme.io/v2")); // non-credential survives
```

Step 3. Validate Fix 2, capping loses identifiers and recovery overflows:

```typescript
const transcriptIds = extractIdentifiersFromMessages([
  { role: "user", content: "deploy hash a1b2c3d4e5f6" },
]);
const oversized = "## Decisions\nKeep flow.\n## Exact identifiers\n"
  + "x".repeat(MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS) + "\na1b2c3d4e5f6";
const capped = capCompactionSummary(oversized);
const lost = computeLostIdentifiers(transcriptIds, capped);
assert(lost.length > 0);  // identifier at tail truncated by capping
const recovered = `${capped}\n\n## Exact identifiers (recovered)\n- ${lost[0]}`;
assert(recovered.length > MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS); // recovery overflows
```

Step 4. Run `npx tsx proof-75336.ts` from the patched branch.

Evidence after fix:

```console
$ cd /tmp/wt-75336 && npx tsx proof-75336.ts

[Proof 1] String-encoded JSON args sanitization
  PASS credential token value is redacted from string args
  PASS non-credential endpoint survives sanitization
  PASS hex identifier extractor does not capture redacted credential
  PASS non-JSON string args pass through for extraction

[Proof 2] Identifier loss detection after capping
  PASS transcript identifiers extracted from messages
  PASS hex identifier normalized to uppercase
  PASS capped summary respects size limit
  PASS identifier at tail is lost after capping
  PASS recovery block exceeds cap (triggers cancel in strict mode)

Results: 9 passed, 0 failed
```

Observed result after fix:

Fix 1: `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers` no longer leaks credential-field values from string-encoded JSON arguments. The `token` field value `deadbeef12345678` is redacted by `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload` before the hex identifier extractor can capture it. Non-credential fields and non-JSON strings pass through normally.

Fix 2: When strict mode detects identifier loss after capping and the recovery block would exceed `MAX_COMPACTION_SUMMARY_CHARS` (16,000 chars), compaction is cancelled with a diagnostic reason instead of returning a summary with known missing identifiers. The cancel reason is stored via `setCompactionSafeguardCancelReason` for downstream diagnostics.

What was not tested: Live compaction with a real model and conversation history (requires a running OpenClaw session with enough context to trigger compaction).

target.title: docs: Document oMLX (Apple Silicon MLX) as memorySearch embedding provider
target.body:
## Summary

Resolves the documentation gap identified in #74732. Adds focused recipes
for using oMLX (Apple Silicon MLX inference server) as a memorySearch
embedding provider via the existing OpenAI-compatible adapter.

## Changes

- `docs/concepts/memory-search.md`: New "Using oMLX" subsection under
  Quick start, plus an oMLX row in the Supported providers table.
- `docs/reference/memory-config.md`: New oMLX example under Remote
  endpoint config, with a warning against the common `provider: "ollama"`
  mistake.
- `docs/providers/openai.md`: New "Local OpenAI-compatible servers"
  subsection under Memory embeddings, surfacing oMLX as a notable
  Apple Silicon use case.

## What this addresses

Per the Codex review on #74732, this is the **narrow docs-only fix**:

> Add a focused docs recipe for oMLX that uses the existing OpenAI-compatible
> embedding path, warns users not to configure oMLX as Ollama, includes the
> local /v1 base URL and model example from the report, and tells users to
> reindex after changing embedding models or vector spaces.

All three points are covered. No core behavior change; no new provider
adapter introduced. The first-class `omlx` adapter discussion (Option B in
#74732) remains separate and is **not** part of this PR.

## Verification

The configuration shipped in this PR was verified end-to-end on:

| Component | Version |
|---|---|
| OpenClaw | 2026.4.26 |
| oMLX | v1.3.6 |
| Embedding model | jina-embeddings-v5-text-small-retrieval-mlx (1024 dims) |

`openclaw memory status --deep` after migration:

- Provider: `openai (requested: openai)`
- Model: `jina-embeddings-v5-text-small-retrieval-mlx`
- Vector dims: `1024`
- Indexed: `150/150 files · 946 chunks`
- Batch failures: `0/2`

## Related

Closes #74732 (docs portion).

cc @jundot — oMLX maintainer, in case the oMLX side wants to cross-link.

target.title: config: accept per-agent elevatedDefault overrides and complete per-agent verbose/elevated flow (#73680)
target.body:
## Summary

Makes a **per-agent `elevatedDefault`** actually work, and completes per-agent
`verboseDefault` flow in the reply path (issue #73680).

## The gap

On current `main`, the runtime already reads a per-agent `elevatedDefault`
(directive resolution + status surfaces) and the docs document it, **but**:
- `AgentEntrySchema` does not include `elevatedDefault`, so config validation
  silently rejects/strips a per-agent `elevatedDefault`.
- `resolveAgentConfig` never populates `elevatedDefault` (unlike `verboseDefault`).
- The reply directive resolution reads only `agentCfg.elevatedDefault` (the global
  default) and `agentCfg.verboseDefault`, skipping the per-agent `agentEntry`
  layer that `reasoningDefault` already uses.

Net effect: a documented per-agent `elevatedDefault` had no effect.

## The fix

- Add `elevatedDefault` to the per-agent config type (`AgentConfig`) and
  `AgentEntrySchema`, with schema help + label entries.
- Resolve it in `resolveAgentConfig` as `entry.elevatedDefault ?? defaults.elevatedDefault`,
  mirroring `verboseDefault`.
- In `resolveCurrentDirectiveLevels` and `resolveReplyDirectives`, read
  `agentEntry.elevatedDefault` (and `agentEntry.verboseDefault`) as the per-agent
  layer between session overrides and the global default — the same precedence
  `reasoningDefault` already uses (`session ?? agentEntry ?? global`). Elevated
  resolution stays gated by `elevatedAllowed`, so this does not widen when elevated
  output is disallowed.
- Regenerate the config doc baseline hash for the new key.

`verboseDefault`'s per-agent schema landed on `main` independently since this PR
was opened; this revision drops the now-redundant schema bits and keeps the still
-missing `elevatedDefault` schema plus the per-agent verbose/elevated fallback in
the directive path. Rebased onto current `main`.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-scope.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.levels.test.ts src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts` → 46 passed (new: per-agent elevated resolution, directive precedence session > agent > global, schema acceptance).
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check` → OK.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:test:src` → clean. `oxfmt --check` / `oxlint` clean.

Closes #73680

target.title: WORKING: All Microsoft Issues and PRs (refresh)

# Microsoft Ecosystem Issues & PRs Tracker

> **Purpose:** Living checklist for maintainers to track all open Microsoft-related issues and PRs (Teams, Windows, WSL, Azure, M365/SharePoint).
>
> **How to use:**
>
> - Mark items resolved by editing this PR body and changing `[ ]` to `[x]`
> - Claim items by adding your GitHub handle to the `Assignee` column
> - Priority guide: **P0** = crash/blocker/security, **P1** = significant bug/regression, **P2** = minor bug/enhancement, **P3** = nice-to-have/stale
> - Items marked _(stale)_ have been flagged by the stale bot due to inactivity
>
> **Last updated:** 2026-06-29 (post-purge audit: refreshed from currently open GitHub issues/PRs and rebuilt from PR #49126 format)

---

## Summary

| Category | Issues | PRs | Total | Closed | Remaining |
| -------- | ------ | --- | ----- | ------ | --------- |
| MS Teams (channel plugin) | 11 | 46 | 57 | 0 | 57 |
| Windows platform | 51 | 41 | 92 | 0 | 92 |
| WSL | 9 | 1 | 10 | 0 | 10 |
| Azure | 8 | 3 | 11 | 0 | 11 |
| SharePoint / M365 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| **Total** | **79** | **91** | **170** | **0** | **170** |

target.title: fix(llm): preserve structured tool result text across providers
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Tool results with structured non-text content were being collapsed into generic placeholders during provider replay and transport conversion. In practice, structured non-media blocks could become `(see attached image)`, `(see attached media)`, or empty/no-output text, which loses useful tool output and can mislead the model.

This PR preserves explicit text first, serializes structured non-media blocks as redacted JSON text, and keeps media-only fallbacks type-aware.


## Current Head

`853b6b41858543bc28b5c5d2725790b93c32d2e9`

## Why This Change Was Made

The canonical user-facing problem is tracked in #96857. The branch already covered core provider replay paths, but the latest ClawSweeper review identified one remaining touched sibling path:

- `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts`: the bundled Google transport still built `functionResponse.response.output` from explicit `text` blocks or a media placeholder only, so structured-only non-media tool results could still become empty output.

This head closes that Google transport gap without importing core `src/**` helpers into plugin production code. The extension now has boundary-safe structured extraction for Google tool results before media/no-output fallback.

## User Impact

- Structured JSON/resource-like tool results remain readable in provider replay instead of being dropped.
- Explicit `text` tool-result blocks still win and are not replaced by serialized structured fallback.
- Media-only tool results still use placeholders, now with the right media type:
  - image-only → `(see attached image)`
  - audio-only → `(see attached audio)`
  - mixed image/audio → `(see attached media)`
- Secret-like fields, inline data URIs, opaque encrypted fields, and binary `data` fields are redacted or omitted before provider-bound replay text is generated.
- The bundled Google transport now matches the core provider replay shape while staying inside the plugin SDK boundary.

## Implementation

- Adds shared provider-side tool-result text extraction for structured blocks.
- Redacts secrets and inline data URIs before serializing structured tool-result blocks.
- Omits opaque/binary fields instead of replaying raw payloads.
- Applies the extraction/fallback path across:
  - OpenAI Responses
  - OpenAI Completions
  - Anthropic provider
  - Google shared provider
  - Mistral provider
  - OpenAI transport stream
  - Anthropic transport stream
- Extends extension fallback handling for:
  - xAI Responses transport normalization
  - Google extension transport stream
- Adds Google extension structured replay repair:
  - structured-only Google tool results serialize into `functionResponse.response.output`
  - explicit text remains text-first
  - media-only blocks remain on media placeholders
  - secret-like, inline-data, opaque, and binary fields are redacted/omitted before replay

## Regression Coverage

Added or updated focused coverage for:

- `src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts`
  - explicit text wins
  - structured non-media blocks serialize as JSON text
  - media-only blocks are omitted from text extraction
  - image/audio/mixed media placeholder selection
  - inline data URI and binary-field redaction
- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts`
  - structured tool-result output is not converted to image placeholder
  - audio-only output uses audio placeholder rather than image/no-output fallback
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
  - structured output is preserved rather than collapsed to image placeholder
- `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts`
  - structured blocks are serialized
  - structured blocks are preserved alongside images
- `extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`
  - structured-only Google tool-result output becomes `functionResponse.response.output` text
  - explicit Google tool-result text wins over structured fallback
  - opaque, binary, inline-data, and secret-like structured fields are redacted/omitted
  - media-only Google tool results stay on media placeholders
- `extensions/xai/stream.test.ts`
  - audio-only function output uses audio placeholder

## Evidence

### Review result addressed

Latest ClawSweeper review on `1ee3b0a60fcdbe4686cc4860bc986e742fb9c483` reported:

- Finding: `[P2] Serialize structured Google tool results before fallback`
- Location: `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts:705-707`
- Root issue: Google transport still extracted only explicit text and otherwise used only a media placeholder, leaving structured-only non-media results as empty `functionResponse.response.output`.

This head addresses that finding with extension-boundary-safe structured extraction and focused Google transport regression coverage.

### Current branch author-side validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot`
  - Result: passed, 1 file / 72 tests.
  - Covers the new Google structured-only replay path, explicit-text-first behavior, media-only placeholders, and redaction/omission behavior.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt extensions/google/transport-stream.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`
  - Result: completed for both changed files.
- `node --check extensions/google/transport-stream.ts`
  - Result: passed before the focused test run.
- `node --check extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`
  - Result: passed before the focused test run.
- `git diff --check`
  - Result: passed before commit.

### Latest patch-quality update

Current head `853b6b41858543bc28b5c5d2725790b93c32d2e9` keeps the existing provider replay evidence intact and addresses the latest ClawSweeper patch-quality/security finding by removing the Google extension-local structured serializer copy:

- `src/plugin-sdk/provider-transport-runtime.ts` now exposes the shared structured tool-result text extraction and media-placeholder helpers as a plugin-safe seam.
- `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts` reuses those shared SDK helpers for Google `functionResponse.response.output`, so Google now follows the same redaction/truncation/media fallback contract as the core provider replay paths.
- `extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts` adds/keeps focused coverage for structured-only Google output, explicit-text-first behavior, media-only placeholders, opaque/binary/inline-data omission, and the reviewed sensitive field names (`privateKey`, `private_key`, `cookie`, `set-cookie`, `jwt`, `session`, `code`, `signature`, generic `key`, and payment fields).

Author-side validation for this head:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot` passed.
- `node --check extensions/google/transport-stream.ts` passed.
- `node --check extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts` passed.
- `node --check src/plugin-sdk/provider-transport-runtime.ts` passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt extensions/google/transport-stream.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-transport-runtime.ts` completed.
- `git diff --check` passed before this update.

Additional review-acceptance validation for this head:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot` passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts src/llm/providers/google-shared.convert.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts src/llm/providers/mistral.test.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts extensions/xai/stream.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot` passed.

### Existing provider replay proof retained from the previous detailed PR body

The previous current-head evidence remains relevant to the core provider replay slice and is repeated here so the PR body stays globally consistent instead of losing proof while the branch advances:

- Current-head Real behavior proof checks previously passed for provider replay/redaction coverage:
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28379436171/job/84077730201>
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28379481457/job/84077892770>
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28379522284/job/84078045029>
- Earlier current-head proof after body refresh also passed:
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28378605470/job/84074771813>
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28378717544/job/84075168883>
- The redacted provider replay artifact demonstrated the current-head replay wire shapes for:
  - OpenAI Responses: structured JSON reached `function_call_output.output` as text instead of `(see attached image)`.
  - Anthropic provider replay: structured tool-result text was serialized into the provider-bound replay content instead of becoming an image placeholder.
  - Mistral provider replay: structured tool-result text was serialized into the provider-bound replay content instead of becoming an image placeholder.
  - Redaction behavior: secret-like input was redacted; inline data URIs, binary data, and opaque encrypted fields were omitted or replaced with redacted placeholders before provider replay.
- A later PR-head Real behavior proof check on `1ee3b0a60fcdbe4686cc4860bc986e742fb9c483` passed before the body-only Evidence-section gate failed:
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28440633789/job/84277435867>

### CI failures observed before this update

- `Real behavior proof` failed because the PR body no longer had an authored `Evidence` section:
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28440711254/job/84277710263>
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28440727959/job/84277767312>
- `QA Smoke CI` failed in `Runtime inventory drift check` with a gateway timeout after `config.patch` and hot reload, not in the provider/media placeholder tests:
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28440636120/job/84277548816>
  - Artifact summary: 80 scenarios passed, 1 failed; failing scenario was `runtime-inventory-drift-check`.

This body update restores authored Evidence, and the code commit retriggers CI for the Google transport repair.

## Risk

Low to moderate:

- Text tool results keep existing explicit text behavior.
- Structured non-media data is serialized with redaction/truncation before provider conversion.
- Media-only outputs still use placeholders, but now use the correct media type instead of defaulting to image.
- Binary payloads and opaque encrypted fields are not replayed as raw text.
- The Google extension implementation stays within plugin SDK boundaries and does not deep-import core provider helpers.

## Rollback

Revert this PR to restore the previous provider/transport placeholder behavior.

target.title: fix(control-ui): share gateway token scope across loopback hosts
target.body:
Repair the existing #43254 branch rather than replacing it. This keeps gateway tokens in sessionStorage, shares token scope only across true same-machine loopback aliases, and preserves protocol, port, and pathname boundaries so distinct local gateways remain isolated.

This repair addresses the hydrated bot findings by avoiding broad 127.* hostname matching, removing misleading unreachable IPv6 handling, covering localhost/127/[::1] same-port sharing, and adding negative coverage for DNS hostnames and different ports.

Credit: original implementation and reproduction analysis by @ssdiwu in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/43254. #69647 is related but tracks a different reverse-proxy path persistence bug and should stay separate. Security-routed linked issues remain out of this fix path.

target.title: fix(media): allow private openai compatible audio transcription endpoints
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Audio media-understanding transcription could target self-hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoints, but audio config did not expose the private-network request opt-in used by guarded provider HTTP.
- Why it matters: Trusted local, RFC1918, or tailnet Whisper-style endpoints need explicit private-network access without globally relaxing media request SSRF policy.
- What changed: Added audio-only request config support for `tools.media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork`, aligned schema/generated metadata/docs, threaded audio request overrides into provider execution, and aligned OpenAI-compatible loopback audio auth with the chat-completion local auth pattern by using the shared `custom-local` marker only for loopback OpenAI-compatible audio bases.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Image and video media request config still reject `allowPrivateNetwork`, private-network access remains disabled by default, explicit `request.auth` still injects the configured auth header, and media/audio does not add the Ollama-specific local-auth branch.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes N/A
- Related N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Audio media config reused the generic media request schema/type path, which rejected `allowPrivateNetwork`, while provider execution needed an audio-specific trusted request override path. The local no-auth path also needed to follow the existing chat-completion pattern: synthesize the shared `custom-local` marker for loopback OpenAI-compatible endpoints, then avoid sending provider-default auth for that marker.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing schema and runner tests did not cover audio-specific private-network request overrides or loopback OpenAI-compatible audio endpoints without provider auth.
- Contributing context (if known): The config docs baseline and audio field metadata are generated/aligned surfaces for this config contract.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/config/schema.test.ts`, `src/media-understanding/openai-compatible-audio.test.ts`, `src/media-understanding/runner.proxy.test.ts`, `src/media-understanding/shared.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Audio config accepts and forwards `allowPrivateNetwork`, image/video reject it, per-model audio request config works, loopback OpenAI-compatible audio can synthesize `custom-local` when provider auth is absent, synthetic local audio auth sends no provider-default `Authorization`, explicit request auth still wins, and Ollama does not get the media/audio local-auth branch.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests exercise the config contract and provider request assembly without needing a live transcription service.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can opt audio transcription requests into private-network endpoints with `tools.media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork` and per-model audio request config. Loopback OpenAI-compatible audio endpoints such as `http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1` can run without provider auth, matching local OpenAI-compatible chat-completion providers. The OpenAI provider docs now show the loopback example base URL.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: The new network capability is an explicit audio-only opt-in for trusted private endpoints; defaults remain blocked by the SSRF guard and image/video still reject the private-network flag. Local no-auth audio is restricted to loopback OpenAI-compatible audio providers using the existing non-secret `custom-local` marker. Explicit request auth still injects the configured header when present.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.1.0-44-cloud-amd64 x86_64
- Runtime/container: Node v22.22.2, pnpm 10.33.0
- Model/provider: OpenAI-compatible audio transcription config path
- Integration/channel (if any): Media understanding audio attachments
- Relevant config (redacted): `tools.media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork`, `tools.media.audio.request.auth`, `tools.media.audio.models[].baseUrl`

### Steps

1. Parse audio, image, and video media config shapes through the runtime zod schema.
2. Run audio provider execution with provider-level, audio-level, and per-model request overrides.
3. Run provider HTTP request assembly with synthetic local auth and explicit request auth.

### Expected

- Audio request config accepts and forwards private-network opt-in only when explicitly configured.
- Image/video reject `allowPrivateNetwork` under media request config.
- Loopback OpenAI-compatible audio can use the shared synthetic local auth marker when provider auth is absent.
- Synthetic local auth does not become a provider-default `Authorization` header.
- Explicit request auth overrides synthetic local auth and injects the configured header.
- Ollama does not receive a media/audio local-auth special case.

### Actual

- Matches expected in local tests and changed-gate validation.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Local validation:

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm test src/media-understanding/openai-compatible-audio.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.proxy.test.ts src/media-understanding/shared.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/model-auth.ts src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts src/media-understanding/runner.proxy.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.test-mocks.ts docs/providers/openai.md docs/nodes/audio.md CHANGELOG.md src/media-understanding/openai-compatible-audio.ts src/media-understanding/openai-compatible-audio.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:changed`

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

Set up a (WhisperLive)[https://github.com/collabora/WhisperLive] server locally. Modified openclaw.json to point to it with `media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork = true`:

```
"media": {
      "audio": {
        "enabled": true,
        "language": "en",
        "models": [
          {
            "provider": "openai",
            "model": "whisper-1",
            "baseUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1",
          }
        ],
        "request": {
          "allowPrivateNetwork": true,
        }
      }
```

Verified audio transcription:

```
$ openclaw capability audio transcribe --file tmp/media-test/transcription-test.wav --language en --json
{
  "ok": true,
  "capability": "audio.transcribe",
  "transport": "local",
  "attempts": [],
  "outputs": [
    {
      "path": "/home/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/tmp/media-test/transcription-test.wav",
      "text": "Open Claw Media Transcription Test. The passphrase is Brass Lobster 7.",
      "kind": "audio.transcription"
    }
  ]
}
```



## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

No PR review conversations currently exist.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes, optional new audio request config only
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Users could point audio transcription at private endpoints they do not fully trust.
  - Mitigation: The private-network exception is explicit, audio-only, disabled by default, documented as trusted-endpoint-only, and covered by schema/runtime tests.
- Risk: Synthetic local auth could accidentally be sent as provider-default auth.
  - Mitigation: OpenAI-compatible audio now uses the shared chat-completion local auth marker and omits provider-default auth for that marker while preserving explicit request auth overrides.

target.title: feat(googlechat): add sessionThread option to bind OpenClaw sessions to Google Chat threads
target.body: AI-assisted: yes (Claude Opus 4.7). ## Summary - Problem: In Google Chat, all messages in a space shared a single OpenClaw session. Starting a new thread to change topic still inherited memory from prior threads, and replies did not reliably land in the user's inbound thread. - Why it matters: Chat threads are the natural topic boundary in Google Chat. Leaking context across threads produces confusing replies ("why does the bot remember something from a different thread?"), forces multiple concurrent threads to queue sequentially on one session, and breaks the mental model users already have from Slack. Requested in #13373. - What changed: Added an opt-in `sessionThread` channel option. When `true`, the OpenClaw session peer id is derived from the inbound thread name, so each Google Chat thread is its own conversation. Replies are now threaded via `thread.name` + `messageReplyOption=REPLY_MESSAGE_FALLBACK_TO_NEW_THREAD`, so bot responses land in the user's thread. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Default behavior is unchanged (`sessionThread: false` keeps space-scoped sessions). No changes to auth, allowlists, webhook routing, DM flow, reactions, or attachments. No slash commands / `/focus`-style bindings added (see "future work" below). ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes #13373 (per-thread session mode feature request) - Closes #41788 (Google Chat replies creating new threads instead of staying in the existing one) - Related #44347 — resolves the first of the two requests in that issue - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ### Note on naming vs the issue's proposal The issue proposes `threadSessionMode: "per-thread" | "per-space"`. This PR ships the same behavior under a boolean flag `sessionThread: boolean` (default `false` = per-space, `true` = per-thread). Rationale: - A boolean matches the two-state nature of the option and is simpler to document. - The name aligns with the existing session-scoping vocabulary in the codebase. - Happy to rename to `threadSessionMode` with a string enum if maintainers prefer the issue's exact wording, it's a straightforward rename. ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: The Google Chat channel used the space id as the session peer id and did not pass the inbound thread name back on outbound messages. That coupling meant (a) all threads in a space mapped to one session, and (b) replies could fall back to a new thread instead of the user's. - Missing detection / guardrail: No peer-id unit coverage for Google Chat; Slack already has `prepare.thread-session-key.test.ts` as prior art for this exact pattern, but nothing equivalent existed for Google Chat. - Contributing context (if known): Google Chat threads were less prominent historically in OpenClaw flows; the space-scoped default was acceptable until users started relying on threads to separate topics. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [ ] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: `extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.session-thread.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: `resolveGoogleChatPeerId` returns the thread name when `sessionThread` is enabled and an inbound thread is present; falls back to `spaceId` otherwise; and produces distinct peer ids for distinct threads so sessions do not cross. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The peer id is the single point where "which session does this message belong to?" is decided for Google Chat. Locking its behavior at the helper boundary catches the regression without pulling in the full monitor stack. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): None for Google Chat. Slack's `prepare.thread-session-key.test.ts` covers the analogous primitive. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - New opt-in config: `channels.googlechat.sessionThread` (default `false`). - `false` (default): space-scoped sessions, prior behavior unchanged. - `true`: one OpenClaw session per Google Chat thread; a new thread starts a fresh conversation. - Outbound messages now consistently target the inbound thread via `thread.name` + `messageReplyOption=REPLY_MESSAGE_FALLBACK_TO_NEW_THREAD`, so replies land in the user's thread more reliably. This applies regardless of `sessionThread`. ## Diagram (if applicable) ```text sessionThread: false (default, unchanged) space S, thread A -> session(peer=S) space S, thread B -> session(peer=S) # shared memory sessionThread: true space S, thread A -> session(peer=A) space S, thread B -> session(peer=B) # isolated memory space S, no thread -> session(peer=S) # falls back to space-scoped ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No - New/changed network calls? No (same Chat API endpoints; only request payload now sets `thread.name` and `messageReplyOption`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? No - Data access scope changed? No (in fact, `sessionThread: true` narrows memory scope per thread) ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS - Runtime/container: Node 22 / local gateway (`pnpm openclaw gateway restart`) - Model/provider: any (provider-agnostic) - Integration/channel (if any): Google Chat - Relevant config (redacted): ```jsonc { "channels": { "googlechat": { "enabled": true, "serviceAccountFile": "/path/to/service-account.json", "sessionThread": true } } } ``` ### Steps 1. In a Google Chat space, mention the bot in thread A and ask an identifying question (e.g. "remember the color red"). 2. Start a new thread B in the same space and ask "what color did I mention?". 3. Reply again inside thread A and ask the same question. ### Expected - `sessionThread: true`: in thread B, the bot has no memory of thread A. In thread A, the bot still remembers "red". Replies land in the thread the user messaged in. - `sessionThread: false` (default): behavior is unchanged from prior releases. ### Actual - Matches expected for both modes. ## Evidence - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after ``` pnpm test extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.session-thread.test.ts ✓ resolveGoogleChatPeerId returns spaceId when sessionThread is disabled ✓ resolveGoogleChatPeerId returns spaceId when sessionThread is undefined (default) ✓ resolveGoogleChatPeerId returns thread name when sessionThread is enabled and inbound has a thread ✓ resolveGoogleChatPeerId falls back to spaceId when sessionThread is enabled but inbound has no thread ✓ resolveGoogleChatPeerId produces distinct peer ids for distinct threads so sessions don't cross Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 5 passed (5) ``` - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - When disabled(previous behavior): - When enabled: - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) - Verified scenarios: - Manually verified in a real Google Chat space against a local gateway: - Bot replies land inside the user's inbound thread (both existing threads and thread-starter messages). - With `sessionThread: true`: new thread = fresh session; prior thread keeps its own context; no cross-thread memory leakage. - With `sessionThread: false`: unchanged, space-scoped memory. - Edge cases checked: - Inbound message with no thread (thread-starter): falls back to space-scoped session as designed. - Two concurrent threads in the same space maintain separate sessions. - What you did **not** verify: - Bot-initiated outbound into a space with no prior inbound thread context (no webhook-origin thread). This PR intentionally does not introduce a `threadKey` fallback for that case; the follow-up work below can add richer binding if demand emerges. - DM flow (no behavior change expected; DM scope is already per-channel-peer). ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? Yes - Config/env changes? Yes, additive only: new optional `channels.googlechat.sessionThread` boolean (default `false`). - Migration needed? No. Existing configs continue to behave as before. - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A. To opt in, set `channels.googlechat.sessionThread: true`. ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: Users relying on cross-thread memory in a space would lose that if they set `sessionThread: true` without realizing the implication. - Mitigation: Default is `false`; docs call out the per-thread isolation explicitly; the name `sessionThread` signals the intent. - Risk: `resolveGoogleChatPeerId` is a new exported helper surface from the plugin's `monitor.ts`. - Mitigation: Kept it narrow (one pure function) and it is only consumed inside the extension itself; no public SDK subpath added. ## Future Work (non-blocking) This PR adds the minimal primitive, matching Slack's auto thread-scoped session behavior, just gated behind an opt-in flag for Google Chat. A follow-up could align with the Telegram/Discord `session.threadBindings` model (`/focus`, `/unfocus`, `/session idle`, `/session max-age`) on top of this primitive. That work requires registering slash commands in the Chat API app config and wiring `SLASH_COMMAND` events, which is out of scope here.

target.title: Fix delivery-ready verification blockers
target.body:
## Summary

This PR closes the remaining delivery-ready verification blockers for the current branch.

It completes three founder-visible fixes:
- suppress non-actionable historical task delivery warning noise on operator surfaces
- normalize heartbeat `0m` semantics across status and health surfaces
- improve async exec approval followup messaging so user-visible replies stop leaking raw internal execution metadata

It also fixes the final type-surface issues discovered during fresh closure verification.

## What changed

### Task registry / operator surface
- stop surfacing already-non-actionable historical succeeded tasks as actionable delivery failure noise
- preserve stored task history while cleaning the reconciled read/audit surface

### Heartbeat status consistency
- align `0m` heartbeat semantics across `status --deep` and `health --json`
- remove contradictory reporting between status surfaces

### Async exec followup messaging
- prefer internal continuation plus synthesized user-facing results
- avoid leaking raw gateway/session/exit metadata into normal chat replies

### Closure verification fixes
- fix the type/test-surface issues found during fresh `pnpm check`
- keep the final closure edits minimal and scoped to the failing surfaces

## Verification

Fresh verification on this branch:
- `pnpm lint` ✅
- `pnpm check` ✅
- `pnpm build` ✅

Previously targeted verification for the repaired slices also passed:
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts` ✅
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-approval-followup.test.ts` ✅
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/commands/health.command.coverage.test.ts src/commands/status-all/diagnosis.test.ts` ✅
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/config/doc-baseline.integration.test.ts test/release-check.test.ts test/openclaw-npm-postpublish-verify.test.ts` ✅

## Notes

An earlier assumption that repo-wide extension lint debt was the remaining blocker did not hold under fresh verification. The real final blockers were a small set of type-surface failures, which are fixed here.

target.title: fix(telegram): durably retry inbound media dropped during restart (#98076)
target.body:
Fixes #98076

## What Problem This Solves

A single inbound media message (e.g. a **document**) whose download fails is
handled best-effort in `processInboundMessage`: it warns "Failed to download
media. Please try again." and lets the update complete. `bot-core` then marks
the **spooled** update `completed: true` (acked). During a gateway
restart/upgrade window the warning send *also* fails, so the document is
**silently dropped** — never written to `media/inbound/`, no retry, no notice to
the user or the assistant. The issue confirms a re-send after the restart landed
correctly, so the loss is restart-window-specific.

## Why This Change Was Made

Inbound media that fails transiently around a restart should be durably re-driven
after the gateway comes back, not acked-and-lost. The spooled-replay completion
contract already keeps `failed-retryable` updates; the single-message media catch
just never recorded that outcome, so the spool acked the update and dropped it.

The retry decision must be precise. `fetch_failed` is the catch-all media error
code and covers both transient failures (network, shutdown/abort) **and**
permanent ones (SSRF/guard denials, local Bot API path/read errors). Durably
retrying a permanent failure would loop it in the spool with the user warning
suppressed. The correct transient/abort determination already lives in
`src/media/fetch.ts` (`isAbortError` / `isTransientNetworkError`), so the policy
belongs there rather than re-derived in the channel.

## User Impact

- A document sent right before a gateway restart is now **downloaded after the
  gateway comes back** instead of silently lost.
- No new user-facing warning spam: the "try again" notice is suppressed only on
  durable spooled replay (we are retrying); live failures and permanent failures
  still warn exactly as before.
- Permanent failures (size limit, blocked/guarded fetch, bad path) are **not**
  requeued — they stay best-effort (acked + warned) and cannot loop the spool.
- Media-group albums are unchanged: they keep their intentional
  skip-the-unfetchable-photo + partial-delivery warning (#55216).

## Evidence

Source-level regression proof, run locally on macOS against this patch (this issue
is `needs-live-repro`; a live restart-during-upload rig and the agentic native
Telegram proof job require maintainer/write perms, so the mechanism is proven
against the real handler + `bot-core` completion frame):

- **`src/media/fetch.ts` — `isDurablyRetryableMediaFetchError`** =
  `shouldRetryMediaFetch` (the canonical in-loop transient policy) **plus**
  shutdown/abort `fetch_failed`. Truth-table unit tests in
  `src/media/fetch.test.ts` cover: transient-network + 408/5xx → retry;
  shutdown/abort `fetch_failed` → retry; **permanent `fetch_failed` (guard/SSRF,
  local path), other 4xx, and size limits → no retry**.
- **Behavioral tests in `bot.create-telegram-bot.channel-post-media.test.ts`**
  drive a real single-message document fetch failure through the actual handler
  inside `runWithTelegramUpdateProcessingFrame` + `withTelegramSpooledReplayUpdate`:
  - spooled-replay **shutdown abort** → `{ kind: "failed-retryable" }` (kept) and
    the retry notice is **not** sent;
  - spooled-replay **permanent `fetch_failed` (guard/SSRF)** → acked (no
    failed-retryable result) and the user **is** warned;
  - spooled-replay **size-limit** → acked + warned.
- **Full telegram + media suites green** (`fetch`, `bot-core`,
  `bot-update-tracker`, `polling-session`, `bot-message`, media-group
  skip-warning, classifier + behavioral): 264 tests pass.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` + `tsgo:core:test` + `tsgo:extensions` +
  `tsgo:extensions:test`, `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`, `oxfmt --check`,
  `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs`, and staged `gitleaks protect --staged --redact`
  all clean.

## Not Covered

A live Telegram account with a gateway restart triggered mid-upload is not
reproduced here; the durable-keep / ack / warning-suppression behavior is asserted
at the source level. A maintainer can capture visible proof via Mantis if desired.

target.title: fix(cli): explain how to recover from device approve deadlock
target.body:
## Problem

When a TUI/device needs a scope upgrade, `openclaw devices approve` could dead-end with no actionable guidance — the core of a genuinely confusing approval experience:

1. **Self-approval deadlock.** The CLI authenticates as the device being upgraded, which lacks `operator.approvals`, so the gateway rejects `device.pair.approve` with `scope upgrade pending approval`. When no loopback local fallback is available (e.g. a remote `--url` gateway), that raw error propagated verbatim with no hint about how to proceed.
2. **Bare `unknown requestId`.** When the id wasn't in pending state (already approved, expired, or superseded by a newer same-device request), the command printed only `unknown requestId`.

## Fix

`src/cli/devices-cli.runtime.ts` (`runDevicesApproveCommand`):
- Catch authorization-type failures (`isApprovalAuthorizationError`) and print actionable guidance: the device can't approve its own scope upgrade (needs `operator.approvals`); rerun with `--token`/`--password` using the gateway owner credentials, or approve from a device that already holds `operator.approvals` (e.g. the Control UI). Reuses the existing `--token`/`--password` reminder.
- Replace bare `unknown requestId` with a message that points to `openclaw devices list` and `openclaw devices approve --latest`.

This pairs with the request-ID churn fix (#98145): with stable ids, the loopback local-admin fallback resolves the common single-box case automatically; this PR makes the *remaining* failure modes (remote gateways, missing ids) self-explanatory.

## Tests

`src/cli/devices-cli.test.ts`:
- Remote `--url` scope-upgrade denial → guidance mentions `operator.approvals`, `--token`, and `openclaw devices list`.
- Superseded/missing id → "No pending request matches" + `openclaw devices approve --latest`, no bare `unknown requestId`.
- Updated the two existing tests that asserted the old `unknown requestId` string.

All 44 tests in the file pass; `oxlint` clean.

## Notes

- No `CHANGELOG.md` edit (per CONTRIBUTING).
- Deliberately does **not** auto-read/use the gateway master token — that's more invasive (SecretRef handling, auth-mode variations) and is better as a separate, carefully-reviewed change; this PR sticks to safe, actionable messaging.
- Companion PRs: request-ID churn (#98145), TUI disconnect copy, `pairing list` empty-enum crash.
- 🤖 AI-assisted (Claude Code), authored/reviewed by @RomneyDa.

target.title: fix(cli): stop `pairing list` crashing with empty channel enum
target.body:
## Problem

Running `openclaw pairing list` with no channel argument when no chat DM pairing channels are configured throws:

```
Channel required. Use --channel <channel> or pass it as the first argument (expected one of: )
```

The empty `(expected one of: )` enum reads like a bug. The deeper UX trap is a **naming collision**: `openclaw pairing` manages *chat DM* pairing, but users reasonably reach for it when trying to approve a **TUI/device or scope-upgrade** request — which actually lives under `openclaw devices`. So they land on an opaque error for a command that was never going to help them.

## Fix

`src/cli/pairing-cli.ts`:
- Guard the supported-channel hint used in help text and error messages so an empty channel list no longer renders a bare `()` / `(expected one of: )` (shows `none configured` instead).
- When no pairing channels are configured, throw an actionable error that redirects to `openclaw devices list` / `openclaw devices approve` rather than failing with an empty enum.

## Tests

`src/cli/pairing-cli.test.ts`:
- `pairing list` with zero configured channels → error mentions `openclaw devices`, never emits `()` or `expected one of: )`, and does not call into the pairing store.
- `pairing list` with multiple channels and no argument → still lists the supported channels (`expected one of: telegram, discord, imessage`).

All 15 tests in the file pass; `oxlint` clean on changed files.

## Notes

- Does not touch `CHANGELOG.md` (per CONTRIBUTING — maintainers/ClawSweeper add entries on landing).
- This is one of a set of fixes for the device scope-upgrade approval flow; the related TUI copy, request-ID churn, and self-approval deadlock are separate PRs.
- 🤖 AI-assisted (Claude Code), authored/reviewed by @RomneyDa.
